tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنا بلوناهم» امتحنا أهل مكة بالقحط والجوع «كما بلونا أصحاب الجنة» البستان «إذ أقسموا ليصرمنَّها» يقطعون ثمرتها «مصبحين» وقت الصباح كي لا يشعر بهم المساكين فلا يعطونهم منها ما كان أبوهم يتصدق به عليهم منها
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّا بَلَوۡنَٰهُمۡ كَمَا بَلَوۡنَآ أَصۡحَٰبَ ٱلۡجَنَّةِ إِذۡ أَقۡسَمُواْ لَيَصۡرِمُنَّهَا مُصۡبِحِينَ (بَلَوْنَاهُمْ: اخْتَبَرْنَاهُمْ, الْجَنَّةِ: الحَدِيقَةِ, لَيَصْرِمُنَّهَا: لَيَقْطَعُنَّ ثِمَارَ حَدِيقَتِهَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami telah mencoba mereka) Kami telah menguji orang-orang musyrik Mekah dengan paceklik dan kelaparan (sebagaimana Kami telah mencoba pemilik-pemilik kebun) atau ladang (ketika mereka bersumpah bahwa mereka sungguh-sungguh akan memetik hasilnya) akan memetik buahnya (di pagi hari) di pagi buta, supaya orang-orang miskin tidak mengetahuinya. Maka orang-orang yang memiliki kebun itu mempunyai alasan bila mereka tidak memberikan sedekah kepada mereka; tidak sebagaimana bapak-bapak mereka yang selalu memberikan sebagian dari hasilnya buat orang-orang miskin sebagai sedekahnya

t.sahih-int: Without making exception
t.asad: and made no allowance [for the will of God]
t.yusuf-ali: But made no reservation, ("If it be Allah's Will)
t.pickthall: And made no exception (for the Will of Allah)
t.arberry: and they added not the saving words
t.hilali-khan: Without saying: Insha' Allah (If Allah will)
t.itani: Without any reservation.
t.mubarakpuri: Without saying: "If Allah wills."
t.qarib: and made no allowance [for the Will of God]
t.wahiduddin: without saying, "If it be God's will
t.shakir: And were not willing to set aside a portion (for the poor)
t.haleem: and made no allowance [for the Will of God]
t.maariful: and did not make any exception (by saying inshā’allāh)
t.khattab: leaving no thought for Allah’s Will
t.ghali: And made no exception (for the Will of Allah)
t.irving: and did not make any reservation about having a second chance
t.iwq: Without making exception

t_id.kemenag: dan mereka tidak menyisihkan (hak fakir miskin),
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya Kami telah menguji penduduk Mekkah dengan memberikan mereka nikmat, lalu mereka mengingkarinya. Ini sama halnya seperti Kami menguji para pemilik kebun ketika mereka bersumpah--tanpa mengingat Allah dan tidak menyerahkan urusan kepada kehendak-Nya--akan memetik buah-buahan dari kebun mereka di pagi hari.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka tidak mengecualikan) di dalam sumpah mereka itu kepada kehendak Allah swt. Ayat ini merupakan jumlah isti'naf atau kalimat permulaan; yakni, kelakuan mereka seperti itu; mereka tidak pernah menggantungkan sumpahnya itu kepada kehendak Allah swt.
t_id.iwq: dan mereka tidak menyisihkan (hak fakir miskin),

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ولا يستثنون» في يمينهم بمشيئة الله تعالى والجملة مستأنفة، أي وشأنهم ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَلَا يَسۡتَثۡنُونَ (وَلَا يَسْتَثْنُونَ: وَلَا يَنْوُونَ اسْتِثْنَاءَ حِصَّةِ المَسَاكِينِ، وَلَمْ يَقُولُوا: إِنْ شَاءَ اللهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan mereka tidak mengecualikan) di dalam sumpah mereka itu kepada kehendak Allah swt. Ayat ini merupakan jumlah isti'naf atau kalimat permulaan; yakni, kelakuan mereka seperti itu; mereka tidak pernah menggantungkan sumpahnya itu kepada kehendak Allah swt

ar: فَطَافَ عَلَیۡهَا طَاۤئِفࣱ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمۡ نَاۤئِمُونَ
ar_simple: فطاف علیها طائف من ربك وهم نائمون

t.sahih-int: So there came upon the garden an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep
t.asad: whereupon a visitation for thy Sustainer came upon that [garden] while they were asleep
t.yusuf-ali: Then there came on the (garden) a visitation from thy Lord, (which swept away) all around, while they were asleep
t.pickthall: Then a visitation from thy Lord came upon it while they slept
t.arberry: Then a visitation from thy Lord visited it, while they were sleeping
t.hilali-khan: Then there passed by on the (garden) something (fire) from your Lord at night and burnt it while they were asleep
t.itani: But a calamity from your Lord went around it while they slept.
t.mubarakpuri: Then there passed over it a Ta'if from your Lord while they were asleep.
t.qarib: But a disaster from your Lord came upon it while they slept
t.wahiduddin: A calamity from your Lord befell the orchard as they slept
t.shakir: Then there encompassed it a visitation from your Lord while they were sleeping
t.haleem: a disaster from your Lord struck the garden as they slept
t.maariful: Then, there whirled around it a whirl (of calamity) from your Lord, while they were asleep
t.khattab: Then it was struck by a torment from your Lord while they slept
t.ghali: Then a (destructive) visitation from your Lord visited (Literally: went around it) it while they were sleeping
t.irving: so a calamity from your Lord came round to them while they were sleeping
t.iwq: Thus there came upon the garden an affliction from your Lord while they were asleep

t_id.kemenag: lalu kebun itu diliputi malapetaka (yang datang) dari Tuhanmu ketika mereka sedang tidur,
t_id.sabiq: Kemudian datang bencana besar dari Tuhanmu pada malam hari ketika mereka terlelap dalam tidur. Kebun-kebun itu menjadi hitam seperti malam yang gelap akibat bencana yang menimpanya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Lalu kebun itu diliputi malapetaka dari Rabbmu) berupa api yang melahap kesemuanya di waktu malam (ketika mereka sedang tidur.)
t_id.iwq: lalu kebun itu diliputi malapetaka (yang datang) dari Tuhanmu ketika mereka sedang tidur,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فطاف عليها طائف من ربك» نار أحرقتها ليلا «وهم نائمون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَطَافَ عَلَيۡهَا طَآئِفٞ مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُمۡ نَآئِمُونَ (فَطَافَ عَلَيْهَا: أَحَاطَ نَازِلًا عَلَيْهَا, طَائِفٌ: نَارٌ أَحْرَقَتْهَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Lalu kebun itu diliputi malapetaka dari Rabbmu) berupa api yang melahap kesemuanya di waktu malam (ketika mereka sedang tidur)

t.sahih-int: And it became as though reaped
t.asad: so that by the morrow it became barren and bleak
t.yusuf-ali: So the (garden) became, by the morning, like a dark and desolate spot, (whose fruit had been gathered)
t.pickthall: And in the morning it was as if plucked
t.arberry: and in the morning it was as if it were a garden plucked
t.hilali-khan: So the (garden) became black by the morning, like a pitch dark night (in complete ruins)
t.itani: And in the morning it was as if picked.
t.mubarakpuri: So by the morning, it became like As-Sarim.
t.qarib: By morning it became barren and bleak
t.wahiduddin: And by morning it lay as if it had already been harvested, a barren land
t.shakir: So it became as black, barren land
t.haleem: and by morning it was stripped bare, a desolate land
t.maariful: Thus, on the next morning, it was like a harvested field
t.khattab: so it was reduced to ashes
t.ghali: So (in the morning) it became as if it were a garden wrested away
t.irving: and one morning it lay as if it had been already harvested
t.iwq: And it became as though reaped

t_id.kemenag: maka jadilah kebun itu hitam seperti malam yang gelap gulita.
t_id.sabiq: Kemudian datang bencana besar dari Tuhanmu pada malam hari ketika mereka terlelap dalam tidur. Kebun-kebun itu menjadi hitam seperti malam yang gelap akibat bencana yang menimpanya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka jadilah kebun itu hitam seperti malam yang gelap gulita) yakni menjadi hangus terbakar semuanya, sehingga tampak hitam.
t_id.iwq: maka jadilah kebun itu hitam seperti malam yang gelap gulita.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فأصبحت كالصريم» كالليل الشديد الظلمة، أي سوداء
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأَصۡبَحَتۡ كَٱلصَّرِيمِ (كَالصَّرِيمِ: كَاللَّيْلِ المُظْلِمِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka jadilah kebun itu hitam seperti malam yang gelap gulita) yakni menjadi hangus terbakar semuanya, sehingga tampak hitam

t.sahih-int: And they called one another at morning
t.asad: Now when they rose at early morn, they called unto one another
t.yusuf-ali: As the morning broke, they called out, one to another
t.pickthall: And they cried out one unto another in the morning
t.arberry: In the morning they called to one another
t.hilali-khan: Then they called out one to another as soon as the morning broke
t.itani: In the morning, they called to one another.
t.mubarakpuri: Then they called out (one to another as soon) as the morning broke.
t.qarib: At day break they called to one another
t.wahiduddin: So they called out to each other at the break of dawn
t.shakir: And they called out to each other in the morning
t.haleem: Still they called each other at daybreak
t.maariful: So, they called out each other as the morning broke
t.khattab: Then by daybreak they called out to each other
t.ghali: Then in the (early) morning they called out to one another
t.irving: Thus they called out to one another next morning
t.iwq: And they called one another at morning

t_id.kemenag: lalu mereka panggil memanggil di pagi hari:
t_id.sabiq: Ketika pagi hari, sebagian dari mereka menyeru yang lainnya, "Pergilah segera ke kebun kalian jika ingin memetik buah-buahan."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Lalu mereka panggil-memanggil di pagi hari.)
t_id.iwq: lalu mereka panggil memanggil di pagi hari:

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فتنادوا مصبحين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَتَنَادَوۡاْ مُصۡبِحِينَ (فَتَنَادَوا: نَادَى بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Lalu mereka panggil-memanggil di pagi hari

ar: أَنِ ٱغۡدُوا۟ عَلَىٰ حَرۡثِكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَـٰرِمِینَ
ar_simple: أن ٱغدوا على حرثكم إن كنتم صرمین

t.sahih-int: [Saying], "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit
t.asad: Go early to your tilth if you want to harvest the fruit
t.yusuf-ali: Go ye to your tilth (betimes) in the morning, if ye would gather the fruits
t.pickthall: Saying: Run unto your field if ye would pluck (the fruit)
t.arberry: Come forth betimes upon your tillage, if you would pluck
t.hilali-khan: Saying: "Go to your tilth in the morning, if you would pluck the fruits
t.itani: “Go early to your plantation, if you are going to harvest.”
t.mubarakpuri: Saying: "Go to your tilth in the morning, if you would pluck (the fruits)."
t.qarib: saying, "Go early to your crop if you want to harvest the fruit
t.wahiduddin: saying, "Be quick to reach your orchard, if you want to gather all your fruits
t.shakir: Saying: Go early to your tilth if you would cut (the produce)
t.haleem: ‘Go early to your field if you wish to gather all its fruits,’
t.maariful: saying, “Set out early, if you are going to pluck (the fruits).”
t.khattab: ˹saying,˺ “Go early to your harvest, if you want to pick ˹all˺ the fruit.”
t.ghali: (Saying), "Go forth early upon your tillage, in case you would wrest away (the fruit)
t.irving: Get out early to your crop if you [want to] harvest it
t.iwq: Saying, "Go early to your crop if you would cut the fruit

t_id.kemenag: "Pergilah diwaktu pagi (ini) ke kebunmu jika kamu hendak memetik buahnya".
t_id.sabiq: Ketika pagi hari, sebagian dari mereka menyeru yang lainnya, "Pergilah segera ke kebun kalian jika ingin memetik buah-buahan."
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Pergilah di waktu pagi ini ke kebun kalian) ke ladang kalian; lafal ini menafsirkan pengertian yang terkandung di dalam lafal tanadauw; atau huruf an dianggap sebagai an mashdariyah (jika kalian hendak memetik buahnya") ingin memetik hasilnya; jawab syaratnya ditunjukkan oleh pengertian kalimat sebelumnya.
t_id.iwq: "Pergilah diwaktu pagi (ini) ke kebunmu jika kamu hendak memetik buahnya".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أن اغدوا على حرثكم» غلتكم تفسير لتنادوا، أو أن مصدرية أي بأن «إن كنتم صارمين» مريدين القطع وجواب الشرط دل عليه ما قبله
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَنِ ٱغۡدُواْ عَلَىٰ حَرۡثِكُمۡ إِن كُنتُمۡ صَٰرِمِينَ (أَنِ اغْدُوا: اذْهَبُوا مُبَكِّرِينَ, حَرْثِكُمْ: مَزْرَعَتِكُمْ, صَارِمِينَ: مُصِرِّينَ عَلَى قَطْعِ الثِّمَارِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pergilah di waktu pagi ini ke kebun kalian) ke ladang kalian; lafal ini menafsirkan pengertian yang terkandung di dalam lafal tanadauw; atau huruf an dianggap sebagai an mashdariyah (jika kalian hendak memetik buahnya") ingin memetik hasilnya; jawab syaratnya ditunjukkan oleh pengertian kalimat sebelumnya

t.sahih-int: So they set out, while lowering their voices
t.asad: Thus they launched forth, whispering unto one another
t.yusuf-ali: So they departed, conversing in secret low tones, (saying)
t.pickthall: So they went off, saying one unto another in low tones
t.arberry: So they departed, whispering together
t.hilali-khan: So they departed, conversing in secret low tones (saying)
t.itani: So off they went, murmuring to one another.
t.mubarakpuri: So they departed, and they were whispering:
t.qarib: So they set out, whispering
t.wahiduddin: So they went off, whispering to one another
t.shakir: So they went, while they consulted together secretly
t.haleem: and went off, whispering
t.maariful: So they set out while they were whispering to each other
t.khattab: So they went off, whispering to one another
t.ghali: So they went off, speaking together in hushed voices
t.irving: They hurried off muttering to one another
t.iwq: Thus they set out, while lowering their voices

t_id.kemenag: Maka pergilah mereka saling berbisik-bisik.
t_id.sabiq: Maka pergilah mereka sambil berbisik-bisik dan berpesan, "Hari ini jangan sampai ada seorang miskin pun yang dapat masuk ke kebun kalian."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka pergilah mereka saling berbisik-bisikan) yakni dengan secara diam-diam.
t_id.iwq: Maka pergilah mereka saling berbisik-bisik.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فانطلقوا وهم يتخافتون» يتسارون
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَٱنطَلَقُواْ وَهُمۡ يَتَخَٰفَتُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka pergilah mereka saling berbisik-bisikan) yakni dengan secara diam-diam

ar: أَن لَّا یَدۡخُلَنَّهَا ٱلۡیَوۡمَ عَلَیۡكُم مِّسۡكِینࣱ
ar_simple: أن لا یدخلنها ٱلیوم علیكم مسكین

t.sahih-int: [Saying], "There will surely not enter it today upon you [any] poor person
t.asad: Indeed, no needy person shall enter it today [and come] upon you [unawares]
t.yusuf-ali: Let not a single indigent person break in upon you into the (garden) this day
t.pickthall: No needy man shall enter it to-day against you
t.arberry: No needy man shall enter it today against your will
t.hilali-khan: No Miskin (poor man) shall enter upon you into it today
t.itani: “No poor person is to enter it upon you today.”
t.mubarakpuri: "No poor person shall enter upon you into it today."
t.qarib: Make sure no poor person enters the garden today
t.wahiduddin: Be sure to stop any poor person from entering the orchard today
t.shakir: Saying: No poor man shall enter it today upon you
t.haleem: ‘Make sure no poor person enters the garden today!’––
t.maariful: saying, “Let no poor man enter into it upon you today.”
t.khattab: “Do not let any poor person enter your garden today.”
t.ghali: (Saying), "Definitely no indigent person shall enter it today against you
t.irving: Don´t admit any needy person in on you today
t.iwq: Saying, "There will surely not enter it today upon you any poor person

t_id.kemenag: "Pada hari ini janganlah ada seorang miskinpun masuk ke dalam kebunmu".
t_id.sabiq: Maka pergilah mereka sambil berbisik-bisik dan berpesan, "Hari ini jangan sampai ada seorang miskin pun yang dapat masuk ke kebun kalian."
t_id.jalalayn-id: ("Pada hari ini janganlah ada seorang miskin pun masuk ke dalam kebun kalian.") Ayat ini merupakan penafsiran dari makna yang terkandung pada ayat sebelumnya; atau huruf an dianggap sebagai huruf mashdariyah.
t_id.iwq: "Pada hari ini janganlah ada seorang miskinpun masuk ke dalam kebunmu".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أن لا يدخلنها اليوم عليكم مسكين» تفسير لما قبله، أو أن مصدرية أي بأن
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَن لَّا يَدۡخُلَنَّهَا ٱلۡيَوۡمَ عَلَيۡكُم مِّسۡكِينٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Pada hari ini janganlah ada seorang miskin pun masuk ke dalam kebun kalian.") Ayat ini merupakan penafsiran dari makna yang terkandung pada ayat sebelumnya; atau huruf an dianggap sebagai huruf mashdariyah

t.sahih-int: And they went early in determination, [assuming themselves] able
t.asad: – and early they went, strongly bent upon their purpose
t.yusuf-ali: And they opened the morning, strong in an (unjust) resolve
t.pickthall: They went betimes, strong in (this) purpose
t.arberry: And they went forth early, determined upon their purpose
t.hilali-khan: And they went in the morning with strong intention, thinking that they have power (to prevent the poor taking anything of the fruits therefrom)
t.itani: And early they went, resolved in intent.
t.mubarakpuri: And they went in the morning with Hard Qadirin.
t.qarib: And early they went bent on their purpose
t.wahiduddin: They set out early in the morning, thinking they had the power to prevent
t.shakir: And in the morning they went, having the power to prevent
t.haleem: they left early, bent on their purpose
t.maariful: And in early hours they rushed quickly, while they were (assuming themselves) powerful (to pluck the fruits and prevent the poor)
t.khattab: And they proceeded early, totally fixated on their purpose
t.ghali: And they went forth early, determined on interdiction
t.irving: So they went off early, grumbling yet ready for work
t.iwq: And they went early in determination, assuming themselves able

t_id.kemenag: Dan berangkatlah mereka di pagi hari dengan niat menghalangi (orang-orang miskin) padahal mereka (menolongnya).
t_id.sabiq: Mereka pergi ke kebun di pagi hari dengan niat buruk dan mengira bahwa mereka akan sangat mampu melaksanakannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan berangkatlah mereka di pagi hari dengan niat menghalangi) orang-orang miskin (seraya merasa mampu) yakni mampu untuk menghalangi orang-orang miskin, menurut dugaan mereka sendiri.
t_id.iwq: Dan berangkatlah mereka di pagi hari dengan niat menghalangi (orang-orang miskin) padahal mereka (menolongnya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وغدوا على حرد» منع للفقراء «قادرين» عليه في ظنهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَغَدَوۡاْ عَلَىٰ حَرۡدٖ قَٰدِرِينَ (عَلَى حَرْدٍ: عَلَى قَصْدِهِمُ السَّيِّئِ فِي مَنْعِ المَسَاكِينِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan berangkatlah mereka di pagi hari dengan niat menghalangi) orang-orang miskin (seraya merasa mampu) yakni mampu untuk menghalangi orang-orang miskin, menurut dugaan mereka sendiri

t.sahih-int: But when they saw it, they said, "Indeed, we are lost
t.asad: But as soon as they beheld [the garden and could not recognize] it, they exclaimed, "Surely we have lost our way
t.yusuf-ali: But when they saw the (garden), they said: "We have surely lost our way
t.pickthall: But when they saw it, they said: Lo! we are in error
t.arberry: But when they saw it, they said, 'Surely we are gone astray
t.hilali-khan: But when they saw the (garden), they said: "Verily, we have gone astray
t.itani: But when they saw it, they said, “We were wrong.
t.mubarakpuri: But when they saw it, they said: "Verily, we have gone astray."
t.qarib: But when they saw it, they said, " We are lost
t.wahiduddin: But when they saw it, they said, "We must have lost our way
t.shakir: But when they saw it, they said: Most surely we have gone astray
t.haleem: but when they saw the garden, they said, ‘We must have lost our way
t.maariful: But when they saw it (the place of the ruined garden), they said, “We have missed the way.”
t.khattab: But when they saw it ˹devastated˺, they cried, “We must have lost ˹our˺ way
t.ghali: Then, as soon as they saw it, they said, "Surely we are indeed erring (people)
t.irving: and when they saw it, they said: "We must be lost
t.iwq: But when they saw it, they said, "Indeed, we are lost

t_id.kemenag: Tatkala mereka melihat kebun itu, mereka berkata: "Sesungguhnya kita benar-benar orang-orang yang sesat (jalan),
t_id.sabiq: Tatkala mereka melihat kebun-kebun itu hitam terbakar, dengan goncang mereka berkata, "Kita sungguh telah tersesat! Ini bukan kebun kita! Atau ini memang kebun kita, tetapi kita telah terhalangi untuk melihatnya."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tatkala mereka melihat kebun itu) dalam keadaan hangus terbakar (mereka berkata, "Sesungguhnya kita benar-benar orang-orang yang sesat.") Bukankah ini kebun kita. Kemudian setelah mereka mengetahui, bahwa itu adalah benar-benar kebun mereka, lalu mereka mengatakan:
t_id.iwq: Tatkala mereka melihat kebun itu, mereka berkata: "Sesungguhnya kita benar-benar orang-orang yang sesat (jalan),

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلما رأوها» سوداء محترقة «قالوا إنا لضالون» عنها، أي ليست هذه ثم قالوا لما علموها
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَمَّا رَأَوۡهَا قَالُوٓاْ إِنَّا لَضَآلُّونَ (لَضَالُّونَ: لَمُخْطِئُونَ فِي طَرِيقِهَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tatkala mereka melihat kebun itu) dalam keadaan hangus terbakar (mereka berkata, "Sesungguhnya kita benar-benar orang-orang yang sesat.") Bukankah ini kebun kita. Kemudian setelah mereka mengetahui, bahwa itu adalah benar-benar kebun mereka, lalu mereka mengatakan

t.sahih-int: Rather, we have been deprived
t.asad: [and then,] "Nay, but we have been rendered destitute
t.yusuf-ali: Indeed we are shut out (of the fruits of our labour)
t.pickthall: Nay, but we are desolate
t.arberry: nay, rather we have been robbed
t.hilali-khan: (Then they said): "Nay! Indeed we are deprived of (the fruits)
t.itani: We are now deprived.”
t.mubarakpuri: "Nay! Indeed we are deprived!"
t.qarib: Rather, we have been ruined
t.wahiduddin: Indeed, we are utterly ruined
t.shakir: Nay! we are made to suffer privation
t.haleem: No- we are ruined!’
t.maariful: (Then, once they realized that the garden is the same, but it has been destroyed, they said,) “No, but we are deprived (of the fruits).”
t.khattab: In fact, we have been deprived ˹of our livelihood˺.”
t.ghali: No indeed, (but) we are dispossessed
t.irving: In fact, we feel destitute
t.iwq: Rather, we have been deprived

t_id.kemenag: bahkan kita dihalangi (dari memperoleh hasilnya)".
t_id.sabiq: Tatkala mereka melihat kebun-kebun itu hitam terbakar, dengan goncang mereka berkata, "Kita sungguh telah tersesat! Ini bukan kebun kita! Atau ini memang kebun kita, tetapi kita telah terhalangi untuk melihatnya."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Bahkan kita dihalangi) dari memperoleh buahnya disebabkan kita telah menghalang-halangi orang-orang miskin dari memperoleh bagiannya.
t_id.iwq: bahkan kita dihalangi (dari memperoleh hasilnya)".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «بل نحن محرومون» ثمرتها بمنعنا الفقراء منها
tafsir.siraj-ar: بَلۡ نَحۡنُ مَحۡرُومُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Bahkan kita dihalangi) dari memperoleh buahnya disebabkan kita telah menghalang-halangi orang-orang miskin dari memperoleh bagiannya

ar: قَالَ أَوۡسَطُهُمۡ أَلَمۡ أَقُل لَّكُمۡ لَوۡلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ
ar_simple: قال أوسطهم ألم أقل لكم لولا تسبحون

t.sahih-int: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt [Allah]
t.asad: Said the most right-minded among them: "Did I not tell you, 'Will you not extol God's limitless glory?’
t.yusuf-ali: Said one of them, more just (than the rest): "Did I not say to you, 'Why not glorify (Allah)
t.pickthall: The best among them said: Said I not unto you: Why glorify ye not (Allah)
t.arberry: Said the most moderate of them, 'Did I not say to you, "Why do you not give glory
t.hilali-khan: The best among them said: "Did I not tell you: why do you not say: Insha' Allah (If Allah will)
t.itani: The most reasonable of them said, “Did I not say to you, ‘if only you would glorify?’”
t.mubarakpuri: The Awsat among them said; "Did I not tell you, `Why do you not Tusabbihun."'
t.qarib: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, that you should praise your Lord's glory
t.wahiduddin: The more upright of the two said, "Did I not bid you to glorify God
t.shakir: The best of them said: Did I not say to you, Why do you not glorify (Allah)
t.haleem: The wisest of them said, ‘Did I not say to you, “Will you not glorify God?”’
t.maariful: Said he who was the best among them, “Did I not say to you, ‘Why do you not pronounce tasbīH (Allah’s purity)?”
t.khattab: The most sensible of them said, “Did I not urge you to say, ‘Allah willing.’?”
t.ghali: Said the most moderate of them, "Did I not say to you: Had you only extolled (Him)
t.irving: Someone more considerate among them said: "Did I not tell you: ´Why do you not glorify [God]?"´
t.iwq: The most moderate of them said, "Did I not say to you, 'Why do you not exalt Allah

t_id.kemenag: Berkatalah seorang yang paling baik pikirannya di antara mereka: "Bukankah aku telah mengatakan kepadamu, hendaklah kamu bertasbih (kepada Tuhanmu)?"
t_id.sabiq: Salah seorang dari mereka, yang paling bijak dan paling baik, mencela mereka seraya berkata, "Bukankah aku telah mengatakan kepada kalian ketika kalian saling berpesan untuk melarang orang-orang miskin, 'Apakah kalian tidak ingat Allah sehingga kalian mengubah niat tersebut'?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah seorang yang paling baik pikirannya di antara mereka) yaitu orang yang terbaik di antara mereka ("Bukankah aku mengatakan kepada kalian, mengapa tidak) kenapa tidak (kalian bertasbih?") kepada Allah seraya bertobat kepada-Nya.
t_id.iwq: Berkatalah seorang yang paling baik pikirannya di antara mereka: "Bukankah aku telah mengatakan kepadamu, hendaklah kamu bertasbih (kepada Tuhanmu)?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال أوسطهم» خيرهم «ألم أقل لكم لولا» هلا «تسبحون» الله تائبين
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ أَوۡسَطُهُمۡ أَلَمۡ أَقُل لَّكُمۡ لَوۡلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ (أَوْسَطُهُمْ: أَعْدَلُهُمْ، وَخَيْرُهُمْ عَقْلًا وَدِينًا, لَوْلَا تُسَبِّحُونَ: هَلَّا تَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ، وَتَسْتَغْفِرُونَهُ؛ مِنْ فِعْلِكُمْ، وَخُبْثِ نِيَّتِكُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Berkatalah seorang yang paling baik pikirannya di antara mereka) yaitu orang yang terbaik di antara mereka ("Bukankah aku mengatakan kepada kalian, mengapa tidak) kenapa tidak (kalian bertasbih?") kepada Allah seraya bertobat kepada-Nya

ar: قَالُوا۟ سُبۡحَـٰنَ رَبِّنَاۤ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَـٰلِمِینَ
ar_simple: قالوا سبحن ربنا إنا كنا ظلمین

t.sahih-int: They said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers
t.asad: They answered: "Limitless in His glory is our Sustainer! Verily, we were doing wrong
t.yusuf-ali: They said: "Glory to our Lord! Verily we have been doing wrong
t.pickthall: They said: Glorified be our Lord! Lo! we have been wrong-doers
t.arberry: They said, 'Glory be to God, our Lord; truly, we were evildoers
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Glory to Our Lord! Verily, we have been Zalimun (wrong-doers, etc)
t.itani: They said, “Glory to our Lord—We were indeed in the wrong.”
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Glory to Our Lord! Verily, we have been wrongdoers."
t.qarib: They said, "May our Lord be exalted in His glory, we were unjust
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Glory be to God, our Lord. We have surely done wrong
t.shakir: They said: Glory be to our Lord, surely we were unjust
t.haleem: they said, ‘Glory be to God, Our Lord! Truly, we were doing wrong!’
t.maariful: They said, “We pronounce the purity of our Lord. No doubt, we were wrongdoers.”
t.khattab: They replied, “Glory be to our Lord! We have truly been wrongdoers.”
t.ghali: They said, All Extolment be to our Lord! Surely we were unjust
t.irving: They said: "Glory be to our Lord; we have been doing (something) wrong
t.iwq: And they said, "Exalted is our Lord! Indeed, we were wrongdoers

t_id.kemenag: Mereka mengucapkan: "Maha Suci Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang zalim".
t_id.sabiq: Setelah sadar, mereka berkata, "Mahasuci Allah dari anggapan kita bahwa Dia telah menzalimi kita dengan musibah ini. Sesungguhnya kitalah yang telah berbuat zalim karena niat buruk kita."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka mengucapkan, "Maha Suci Rabb kami, sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang zalim.") Karena kami telah menghalang-halangi orang-orang miskin dari haknya.
t_id.iwq: Mereka mengucapkan: "Maha Suci Tuhan kami, sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang zalim".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا سبحان ربنا إنا كنا ظالمين» بمنع الفقراء حقهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ سُبۡحَٰنَ رَبِّنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka mengucapkan, "Maha Suci Rabb kami, sesungguhnya kami adalah orang-orang yang zalim.") Karena kami telah menghalang-halangi orang-orang miskin dari haknya

t.sahih-int: Then they approached one another, blaming each other
t.asad: and then they turned upon one another with mutual reproaches
t.yusuf-ali: Then they turned, one against another, in reproach
t.pickthall: Then some of them drew near unto others, self-reproaching
t.arberry: And they advanced one upon another, blaming each other
t.hilali-khan: Then they turned, one against another, in blaming
t.itani: Then they turned to one another, blaming one another.
t.mubarakpuri: Then they turned one against another, blaming.
t.qarib: Then they turned to one another, each blaming the other
t.wahiduddin: Then they began to heap reproaches on each other
t.shakir: Then some of them advanced against others, blaming each other
t.haleem: and then they turned to each other in mutual reproach
t.maariful: Then, (at the beginning,) they started reproaching one another
t.khattab: Then they turned on each other, throwing blame
t.ghali: Then some of them came forward to others, blaming each other
t.irving: Still some of them pounced on others, blaming one another
t.iwq: Then they approached one another, blaming each other

t_id.kemenag: Lalu sebahagian mereka menghadapi sebahagian yang lain seraya cela mencela.
t_id.sabiq: Masing-masing dari mereka berhadap-hadapan saling mencela dan berkata, "Celaka! Sesungguhnya kezaliman kita telah melampaui batas berlebihan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Lalu sebagian mereka menghadapi sebagian yang lain seraya cela-mencela.)
t_id.iwq: Lalu sebahagian mereka menghadapi sebahagian yang lain seraya cela mencela.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فأقبل بعضهم على بعض يتلاومون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأَقۡبَلَ بَعۡضُهُمۡ عَلَىٰ بَعۡضٖ يَتَلَٰوَمُونَ (يَتَلَاوَمُونَ: يَلُومُ بَعْضُهُمْ بَعْضًا عَلَى مَا قَصَدُوهُ مِنْ مَنْعٍ لِلْمَسَاكِينِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Lalu sebagian mereka menghadapi sebagian yang lain seraya cela-mencela

t.sahih-int: They said, "O woe to us; indeed we were transgressors
t.asad: [In the end] they said: "Oh, woe unto us! Verily, we did behave outrageously
t.yusuf-ali: They said: "Alas for us! We have indeed transgressed
t.pickthall: They said: Alas for us! In truth we were outrageous
t.arberry: They said, 'Woe, alas for us! Truly, we were insolent
t.hilali-khan: They said: "Woe to us! Verily, we were Taghun (transgressors and disobedient, etc)
t.itani: They said, “Woe to us—we were indeed domineering.
t.mubarakpuri: They said: "Woe to us! Verily, we were Taghin."
t.qarib: They said, "How terrible for us; we were aggressors
t.wahiduddin: They said, "Alas for us, our behaviour was beyond the pale
t.shakir: Said they: O woe to us! surely we were inordinate
t.haleem: They said, ‘Alas for us! We have done terrible wrong
t.maariful: (and at last,) they said, “Woe to us! In fact, we (all) were outrageous
t.khattab: They said, “Woe to us! We have certainly been transgressors
t.ghali: They said, "O woe to us! Surely we were inordinate
t.irving: they said: "It´s too bad for us; we have been so arrogant
t.iwq: They said, "O woe to us; verily we were transgressors

t_id.kemenag: Mereka berkata: "Aduhai celakalah kita; sesungguhnya kita ini adalah orang-orang yang melampaui batas".
t_id.sabiq: Masing-masing dari mereka berhadap-hadapan saling mencela dan berkata, "Celaka! Sesungguhnya kezaliman kita telah melampaui batas berlebihan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Aduhai) huruf ya di sini bermakna tanbih (celakalah kita) binasalah kita (sesungguhnya kita ini benar-benar orang-orang yang melampaui batas.)
t_id.iwq: Mereka berkata: "Aduhai celakalah kita; sesungguhnya kita ini adalah orang-orang yang melampaui batas".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالوا يا» للتنبيه «ويلنا» هلا كنا «إنا كنا طاغين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالُواْ يَٰوَيۡلَنَآ إِنَّا كُنَّا طَٰغِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mereka berkata, "Aduhai) huruf ya di sini bermakna tanbih (celakalah kita) binasalah kita (sesungguhnya kita ini benar-benar orang-orang yang melampaui batas)

ar: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَاۤ أَن یُبۡدِلَنَا خَیۡرࣰا مِّنۡهَاۤ إِنَّاۤ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رَ ٰغِبُونَ
ar_simple: عسى ربنا أن یبدلنا خیرا منها إنا إلى ربنا ر غبون

t.sahih-int: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us [one] better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous
t.asad: [But] it may be that our Sustainer will grant us something better instead: for, verily, unto our Sustainer do we turn with hope
t.yusuf-ali: It may be that our Lord will give us in exchange a better (garden) than this: for we do turn to Him (in repentance)
t.pickthall: It may be that our Lord will give us better than this in place thereof. Lo! we beseech our Lord
t.arberry: It may be that our Lord will give us in exchange a better than it; to our Lord we humbly turn
t.hilali-khan: We hope that our Lord will give us in exchange a better (garden) than this. Truly, we turn to our Lord (wishing for good that He may forgive our sins, and reward us in the Hereafter)
t.itani: Perhaps our Lord will give us a better substitute for it. We are turning to our Lord.”
t.mubarakpuri: "We hope that our Lord will give us in exchange better than it. Truly, we hope in our Lord."
t.qarib: Perhaps our Lord will give us a better substitute for it. We are turning to our Lord in hope
t.wahiduddin: May be our Lord will give us a better orchard in its stead; we turn to Him
t.shakir: Maybe, our Lord will give us instead one better than it; surely to our Lord do we make our humble petition
t.haleem: but maybe our Lord will give us something better in its place: we truly turn to Him in hope.’
t.maariful: We hope that our Lord will give us in exchange something better than this. Truly we turn to Allah.”
t.khattab: We trust our Lord will give us a better garden than this, ˹for˺ we are indeed turning to our Lord with hope.”
t.ghali: It may be that our Lord will give us in exchange a more charitable (i.e., better) one than it; surely to our Lord are we desirous (of His Grace)
t.irving: Perhaps our Lord will exchange it for something even better than what we already have. We will plead with our Lord
t.iwq: Perhaps our Lord will substitute for us one better than it. Indeed, we are toward our Lord desirous

t_id.kemenag: Mudah-mudahan Tuhan kita memberikan ganti kepada kita dengan (kebun) yang lebih baik daripada itu; sesungguhnya kita mengharapkan ampunan dari Tuhan kita.
t_id.sabiq: Semoga Allah mengganti kebun kita dengan yang lebih baik. Sesungguhsnya hanya kepada Tuhan kita sajalah kita mengharap ampunan dan ganti."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mudah-mudahan Rabb kita memberikan ganti kepada kita) dapat dibaca yubdilanaa dan yubaddilanaa (yang lebih baik daripada itu; sesungguhnya kita mengharapkan ampunan dari Rabb kita.") Supaya Dia menerima tobat kita dan mendatangkan kepada kita kebun yang lebih baik dari kebun kita yang dahulu. Menurut suatu riwayat disebutkan, bahwa setelah itu mereka diberi kebun yang lebih baik dari yang semula.
t_id.iwq: Mudah-mudahan Tuhan kita memberikan ganti kepada kita dengan (kebun) yang lebih baik daripada itu; sesungguhnya kita mengharapkan ampunan dari Tuhan kita.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «عسى ربنا أن يبدِّلنا» بالتشديد والتخفيف «خيرا منها إنا إلى ربنا راغبون» ليقبل توبتنا ويرد علينا خيرا من جنتنا، روي أنهم أُبدلوا خيرا منها
tafsir.siraj-ar: عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَآ أَن يُبۡدِلَنَا خَيۡرٗا مِّنۡهَآ إِنَّآ إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رَٰغِبُونَ (رَاغِبُونَ: طَالِبُونَ الخَيْرَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mudah-mudahan Rabb kita memberikan ganti kepada kita) dapat dibaca yubdilanaa dan yubaddilanaa (yang lebih baik daripada itu; sesungguhnya kita mengharapkan ampunan dari Rabb kita.") Supaya Dia menerima tobat kita dan mendatangkan kepada kita kebun yang lebih baik dari kebun kita yang dahulu. Menurut suatu riwayat disebutkan, bahwa setelah itu mereka diberi kebun yang lebih baik dari yang semula

ar: كَذَ ٰلِكَ ٱلۡعَذَابُۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلۡءَاخِرَةِ أَكۡبَرُۚ لَوۡ كَانُوا۟ یَعۡلَمُونَ
ar_simple: كذ لك ٱلعذاب ولعذاب ٱلءاخرة أكبر لو كانوا یعلمون

t.sahih-int: Such is the punishment [of this world]. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew
t.asad: SUCH is the suffering [with which We try some people in this world]; but greater by far will be the suffering [which sinners shall have to bear] in the life to come - if they but knew it
t.yusuf-ali: Such is the Punishment (in this life); but greater is the Punishment in the Hereafter,- if only they knew
t.pickthall: Such was the punishment. And verily the punishment of the Hereafter is greater if they did but know
t.arberry: Such is the chastisement; and the chastisement of the world to come, is assuredly greater, did they but know
t.hilali-khan: Such is the punishment (in this life), but truly, the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they but knew
t.itani: Such is the punishment; but the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew.
t.mubarakpuri: Such is the punishment, but truly, the punishment of the Hereafter is greater if they but knew.
t.qarib: Such is the punishment. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew
t.wahiduddin: Such was their punishment, [in this life]. But the punishment of the life to come is much more severe, if only they knew it
t.shakir: Such is the chastisement, and certainly the chastisement of the hereafter is greater, did they but know
t.haleem: Such is the punishment [in this life], but greater still is the punishment in the Hereafter, if only they knew
t.maariful: In this way the punishment comes. And, of course, the punishment of the Hereafter is even greater, if they but realize
t.khattab: That is the ˹way of Our˺ punishment ˹in this world˺. But the punishment of the Hereafter is certainly far worse, if only they knew
t.ghali: Thus was the torment; and the torment of the Hereafter is indeed greater, if they did know
t.irving: Such is torment, although torment in the Hereafter will be even greater if they only realized it
t.iwq: Such is the punishment of this world. And the punishment of the Hereafter is greater, if they only knew

t_id.kemenag: Seperti itulah azab (dunia). Dan sesungguhnya azab akhirat lebih besar jika mereka mengetahui.
t_id.sabiq: Seperti musibah yang terjadi di kebun itulah siksa yang akan Aku turunkan ke dunia untuk orang yang berhak menerimanya. Dan siksa akhirat sungguh lebih besar lagi kalau saja manusia mengetahuinya
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Seperti itulah) sebagaimana azab Kami kepada mereka (azab) di dunia, bagi orang yang menentang perintah Kami dari kalangan orang-orang kafir Mekah dan lain-lainnya. (Dan sesungguhnya azab akhirat lebih besar jika mereka mengetahui) jika mereka mengetahuinya, niscaya mereka tidak akan menentang perintah Kami. Ayat ini diturunkan sewaktu orang-orang kafir Mekah mengatakan, bahwa jika Dia membangkitkan kami, niscaya kami akan diberi pahala yang lebih baik daripada kalian.
t_id.iwq: Seperti itulah azab (dunia). Dan sesungguhnya azab akhirat lebih besar jika mereka mengetahui.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «كذلك» أي مثل العذاب لهؤلاء «العذاب» لمن خالف أمرنا من كفار مكة وغيرهم «ولعذاب الآخرة أكبر لو كانوا يعلمون» عذابها ما خالفوا أمرنا، ونزل لما قالوا إن بعثنا نعطى أفضل منكم
tafsir.siraj-ar: كَذَٰلِكَ ٱلۡعَذَابُۖ وَلَعَذَابُ ٱلۡأٓخِرَةِ أَكۡبَرُۚ لَوۡ كَانُواْ يَعۡلَمُونَ (كَذَلِكَ الْعَذَابُ: مِثْلَ ذَلِكَ العِقَابِ الَّذِي عَاقَبْنَاهُمْ بِهِ نُعَاقِبُ كُلَّ مَنْ بَخِلَ، وَخَالَفَ أَمْرَ اللهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Seperti itulah) sebagaimana azab Kami kepada mereka (azab) di dunia, bagi orang yang menentang perintah Kami dari kalangan orang-orang kafir Mekah dan lain-lainnya. (Dan sesungguhnya azab akhirat lebih besar jika mereka mengetahui) jika mereka mengetahuinya, niscaya mereka tidak akan menentang perintah Kami. Ayat ini diturunkan sewaktu orang-orang kafir Mekah mengatakan, bahwa jika Dia membangkitkan kami, niscaya kami akan diberi pahala yang lebih baik daripada kalian

ar: إِنَّ لِلۡمُتَّقِینَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمۡ جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِیمِ
ar_simple: إن للمتقین عند ربهم جنت ٱلنعیم

t.sahih-int: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure
t.asad: For, behold, it is the God-conscious [alone] whom gardens of bliss await with their Sustainer
t.yusuf-ali: Verily, for the Righteous, are Gardens of Delight, in the Presence of their Lord
t.pickthall: Lo! for those who keep from evil are gardens of bliss with their Lord
t.arberry: Surely for the godfearing shall be Gardens of Bliss with their Lord
t.hilali-khan: Verily, for the Muttaqun (pious and righteous persons - see V. 2:2) are Gardens of delight (Paradise) with their Lord
t.itani: For the righteous are Gardens of Delight with their Lord.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, for those who have Taqwa are Gardens of Delight with their Lord.
t.qarib: There will be Gardens of delight for those who are mindful of God
t.wahiduddin: Those who are mindful of their Lord will be rewarded with gardens of bliss
t.shakir: Surely those who guard (against evil) shall have with their Lord gardens of bliss
t.haleem: There will be Gardens of bliss for those who are mindful of God
t.maariful: Surely, for the God-fearing there are gardens of bliss
t.khattab: Indeed, the righteous will have the Gardens of Bliss with their Lord
t.ghali: Surely for the pious will be the Gardens of Bliss in the Providence of their Lord
t.irving: The heedful will have gardens of bliss alongside their Lord
t.iwq: Indeed, for the righteous with their Lord are the Gardens of Pleasure

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa (disediakan) surga-surga yang penuh kenikmatan di sisi Tuhannya.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang bertakwa akan memperoleh surga-surga yang penuh kenikmatan di sisi Tuhan mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa surga-surga yang penuh kenikmatan di sisi Rabbnya.)
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa (disediakan) surga-surga yang penuh kenikmatan di sisi Tuhannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن للمتقين عند ربهم جنات النعيم»
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ لِلۡمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمۡ جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Sesungguhnya bagi orang-orang yang bertakwa surga-surga yang penuh kenikmatan di sisi Rabbnya

t.sahih-int: Then will We treat the Muslims like the criminals
t.asad: or should We, perchance, treat those who surrender themselves unto Us as [We would treat] those who remain lost in sin
t.yusuf-ali: Shall We then treat the People of Faith like the People of Sin
t.pickthall: Shall We then treat those who have surrendered as We treat the guilty
t.arberry: What, shall we make those who have surrendered like to the sinners
t.hilali-khan: Shall We then treat the (submitting) Muslims like the Mujrimun (criminals, polytheists and disbelievers, etc)
t.itani: Shall We treat the Muslims like the villains?
t.mubarakpuri: Shall We then treat the Muslims like the criminals
t.qarib: Would We treat those who submit to us like those who forced others to reject Our messages
t.wahiduddin: Should We treat the true believers and the wrongdoers alike
t.shakir: What! shall We then make (that is, treat) those who submit as the guilty
t.haleem: Should We treat those who submit to Us as We treat those who do evil
t.maariful: Otherwise, shall We make the obedient like the sinners
t.khattab: Should We then treat those who have submitted like the wicked
t.ghali: Shall We then make the Muslims (Literally: the ones who have surrendered to Allah) like to the criminals
t.irving: Are We to treat Muslims as if they were criminals
t.iwq: Then will We treat the Muslims like the criminals

t_id.kemenag: Maka apakah patut Kami menjadikan orang-orang Islam itu sama dengan orang-orang yang berdosa (orang kafir)?
t_id.sabiq: Apakah Kami berlaku zalim dalam memberi keputusan dengan menyamakan antara orang-orang Muslim dan orang-orang kafir? Apa yang telah terjadi pada kalian? Bagaimana mungkin kalian membuat keputusan yang zalim seperti itu?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka apakah patut Kami menjadikan orang-orang Islam itu sama dengan orang-orang yang berdosa?) maksudnya mendapatkan pahala yang sama dengan orang-orang kafir.
t_id.iwq: Maka apakah patut Kami menjadikan orang-orang Islam itu sama dengan orang-orang yang berdosa (orang kafir)?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أفنجعل المسلمين كالمجرمين» أي تابعين لهم في العطاء
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَفَنَجۡعَلُ ٱلۡمُسۡلِمِينَ كَٱلۡمُجۡرِمِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka apakah patut Kami menjadikan orang-orang Islam itu sama dengan orang-orang yang berdosa?) maksudnya mendapatkan pahala yang sama dengan orang-orang kafir

t.sahih-int: What is [the matter] with you? How do you judge
t.asad: What is amiss with you? On what do you base your judgment [of right and wrong]
t.yusuf-ali: What is the matter with you? How judge ye
t.pickthall: What aileth you? How foolishly ye judge
t.arberry: What ails you then, how you judge
t.hilali-khan: What is the matter with you? How judge you
t.itani: What is the matter with you? How do you judge?
t.mubarakpuri: What is the matter with you How judge you
t.qarib: What is the matter with you? How do you judge
t.wahiduddin: What ails you? How ill you judge
t.shakir: What has happened to you? How do you judge
t.haleem: What is the matter with you? On what basis do you judge
t.maariful: What has happened to you? How do you judge
t.khattab: What is the matter with you? How do you judge
t.ghali: What is it with you? How do you judge
t.irving: What is wrong with you? How do you decide matters
t.iwq: What is the matter with you? How do you judge

t_id.kemenag: Atau adakah kamu (berbuat demikian): bagaimanakah kamu mengambil keputusan?
t_id.sabiq: Apakah Kami berlaku zalim dalam memberi keputusan dengan menyamakan antara orang-orang Muslim dan orang-orang kafir? Apa yang telah terjadi pada kalian? Bagaimana mungkin kalian membuat keputusan yang zalim seperti itu?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Mengapa kalian berbuat demikian; bagaimanakah kalian mengambil keputusan?) dengan keputusan yang rusak ini?
t_id.iwq: Atau adakah kamu (berbuat demikian): bagaimanakah kamu mengambil keputusan?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ما لكم كيف تحكمون» هذا الحكم الفاسد
tafsir.siraj-ar: مَا لَكُمۡ كَيۡفَ تَحۡكُمُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Mengapa kalian berbuat demikian; bagaimanakah kalian mengambil keputusan?) dengan keputusan yang rusak ini

t.sahih-int: Or do you have a scripture in which you learn
t.asad: Or have you, perchance, a [special] divine writ which you study
t.yusuf-ali: Or have ye a book through which ye learn
t.pickthall: Or have ye a scripture wherein ye learn
t.arberry: Or have you a Book wherein you study
t.hilali-khan: Or have you a Book through which you learn
t.itani: Or do you have a scripture in which you study.
t.mubarakpuri: Or have you a Book wherein you learn,
t.qarib: Or do you have a Book which you study
t.wahiduddin: Have you a Scripture that tells you
t.shakir: Or have you a book wherein you read
t.haleem: Do you have a Scripture that tells you
t.maariful: Do you have a book in which you read
t.khattab: Or do you have a scripture, in which you read
t.ghali: Or even do you have a Book wherein you study
t.irving: Or do you have some book to study from
t.iwq: Or do you have a scripture in which you learn

t_id.kemenag: Atau adakah kamu mempunyai sebuah kitab (yang diturunkan Allah) yang kamu membacanya?,
t_id.sabiq: Atau apakah kalian mempunyai kitab suci dari Allah yang di dalamnya kalian baca bahwa kalian mendapatkan apa yang kalian pilih?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau adakah) artinya, benarkah (kalian mempunyai kitab) yang diturunkan dari Allah (yang kalian mempelajarinya) yang kalian membacanya.
t_id.iwq: Atau adakah kamu mempunyai sebuah kitab (yang diturunkan Allah) yang kamu membacanya?,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم» أي بل أ «لكم كتاب» منزل «فيه تدرسون» أي تقرؤون
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ لَكُمۡ كِتَٰبٞ فِيهِ تَدۡرُسُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau adakah) artinya, benarkah (kalian mempunyai kitab) yang diturunkan dari Allah (yang kalian mempelajarinya) yang kalian membacanya

t.sahih-int: That indeed for you is whatever you choose
t.asad: and in which you find all that you may wish to find
t.yusuf-ali: That ye shall have, through it whatever ye choose
t.pickthall: That ye shall indeed have all that ye choose
t.arberry: Surely therein you shall have whatever you choose
t.hilali-khan: That you shall have all that you choose
t.itani: Wherein there is whatever you choose?
t.mubarakpuri: That you shall therein have all that you choose
t.qarib: And in it do you find all that you wish for
t.wahiduddin: that you will be granted whatever you choose
t.shakir: That you have surely therein what you choose
t.haleem: that you will be granted whatever you choose
t.maariful: that for you there is what you choose
t.khattab: that you will have whatever you choose
t.ghali: Surely therein you will have whatever you freely choose
t.irving: Do you have whatever you select from it
t.iwq: That verily for you is whatever you choose

t_id.kemenag: bahwa di dalamnya kamu benar-benar boleh memilih apa yang kamu sukai untukmu.
t_id.sabiq: Atau apakah kalian mempunyai kitab suci dari Allah yang di dalamnya kalian baca bahwa kalian mendapatkan apa yang kalian pilih?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Bahwa di dalamnya kalian benar-benar boleh memilih apa yang kalian sukai) kalian boleh memilih sesuka hati.
t_id.iwq: bahwa di dalamnya kamu benar-benar boleh memilih apa yang kamu sukai untukmu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إن لكم فيه لما تخيرون» تختارون
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّ لَكُمۡ فِيهِ لَمَا تَخَيَّرُونَ (تَخَيَّرُونَ: تَشْتَهُونَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Bahwa di dalamnya kalian benar-benar boleh memilih apa yang kalian sukai) kalian boleh memilih sesuka hati

ar: أَمۡ لَكُمۡ أَیۡمَـٰنٌ عَلَیۡنَا بَـٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ یَوۡمِ ٱلۡقِیَـٰمَةِ إِنَّ لَكُمۡ لَمَا تَحۡكُمُونَ
ar_simple: أم لكم أیمن علینا بلغة إلى یوم ٱلقیمة إن لكم لما تحكمون

t.sahih-int: Or do you have oaths [binding] upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge
t.asad: Or have you received a solemn promise, binding on Us till Resurrection Day, that yours will assuredly be whatever you judge [to be your rightful due]
t.yusuf-ali: Or have ye Covenants with Us to oath, reaching to the Day of Judgment, (providing) that ye shall have whatever ye shall demand
t.pickthall: Or have ye a covenant on oath from Us that reacheth to the Day of Judgment, that yours shall be all that ye ordain
t.arberry: Or have you oaths from Us, reaching to the Day of Resurrection? Surely you shall have whatever you judge
t.hilali-khan: Or you have oaths from Us, reaching to the Day of Resurrection that yours will be what you judge
t.itani: Or do you have oaths from Us, binding until the Day of Resurrection, that you will have whatever you demand?
t.mubarakpuri: Or have you oaths from Us, lasting until the Day of Judgement, that yours will be what you judge
t.qarib: Or do you have oaths from Us, that are binding until the Day of Resurrection, allowing you to have whatever you want
t.wahiduddin: Or do you have Our solemn oaths, binding upon Us till the Day of Resurrection, that you shall have whatever you yourselves decide
t.shakir: Or have you received from Us an agreement confirmed by an oath extending to the day of resurrection that you shall surely have what you demand
t.haleem: Have you received from Us solemn oaths, binding to the Day of Resurrection, that you will get whatever you yourselves decide
t.maariful: Or do you have oaths sworn by Us, remaining effective up to the Day of Judgment, that you will get what you decide
t.khattab: Or do you have oaths binding on Us until the Day of Judgment that you will have whatever you decide
t.ghali: Or even do you have oaths from Us, reaching to the Day of the Resurrection? Surely you will have whatever you judge
t.irving: Or do you have some pledge binding on Us until Resurrection Day that you shall have whatever you decide upon
t.iwq: Or do you have oaths binding upon Us, extending until the Day of Resurrection, that indeed for you is whatever you judge

t_id.kemenag: Atau apakah kamu memperoleh janji yang diperkuat dengan sumpah dari Kami, yang tetap berlaku sampai hari kiamat; sesungguhnya kamu benar-benar dapat mengambil keputusan (sekehendakmu)?
t_id.sabiq: Atau apakah kalian mempunyai janji-janji atas Kami yang diperkuat dengan sumpah yang berlaku sampai hari kiamat? Sesungguhnya kalian benar-benar dapat mengambil keputusan sekehendak kalian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau apakah kalian memperoleh janji-janji) perjanjian-perjanjian (yang diperkuat dengan sumpah dari Kami) yang telah diperkuat oleh Kami (yang tetap berlaku sampai hari kiamat) lafal ilaa yaumil qiyaamah ini bertaalluq kepadanya makna yang terkandung di dalam lafal 'alainaa, dan di dalam kalam ini terkandung makna qasam atau sumpah, yakni Kami telah bersumpah untuk mewajibkannya bagi kalian (sesungguhnya kalian benar-benar dapat mengambil keputusan) sekehendak kalian tentangnya?
t_id.iwq: Atau apakah kamu memperoleh janji yang diperkuat dengan sumpah dari Kami, yang tetap berlaku sampai hari kiamat; sesungguhnya kamu benar-benar dapat mengambil keputusan (sekehendakmu)?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم لكم أَيمان» عهود «علينا بالغة» واثقة «إلى يوم القيامة» متعلق معنى بعلينا، وفي هذا الكلام معنى القسم، أي أقسمنا لكم وجوابه «إن لكم لما تحكمون» به لأنفسكم
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ لَكُمۡ أَيۡمَٰنٌ عَلَيۡنَا بَٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَوۡمِ ٱلۡقِيَٰمَةِ إِنَّ لَكُمۡ لَمَا تَحۡكُمُونَ (أَيْمَانٌ: عُهُودٌ، وَمَوَاثِيقُ, إِنَّ لَكُمْ لَمَا تَحْكُمُونَ: إِنَّهُ سَيَحْصُلُ لَكُمْ مَا تُرِيدُونَ، وَتَشْتَهُونَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau apakah kalian memperoleh janji-janji) perjanjian-perjanjian (yang diperkuat dengan sumpah dari Kami) yang telah diperkuat oleh Kami (yang tetap berlaku sampai hari kiamat) lafal ilaa yaumil qiyaamah ini bertaalluq kepadanya makna yang terkandung di dalam lafal 'alainaa, dan di dalam kalam ini terkandung makna qasam atau sumpah, yakni Kami telah bersumpah untuk mewajibkannya bagi kalian (sesungguhnya kalian benar-benar dapat mengambil keputusan) sekehendak kalian tentangnya

t.sahih-int: Ask them which of them, for that [claim], is responsible
t.asad: Ask them which of them is able to vouch for this
t.yusuf-ali: Ask thou of them, which of them will stand surety for that
t.pickthall: Ask them (O Muhammad) which of them will vouch for that
t.arberry: Ask them, which of them will guarantee that
t.hilali-khan: Ask them, which of them will stand surety for that
t.itani: Ask them, which of them will guarantee that.
t.mubarakpuri: Ask them, which of them will stand as surety for that!
t.qarib: Ask them which of them, will be responsible for that claim
t.wahiduddin: Ask them, which of them will vouch for that
t.shakir: Ask them which of them will vouch for that
t.haleem: Ask them [Prophet] which of them will guarantee this
t.maariful: Ask them which of them stands surety for that
t.khattab: Ask them ˹O Prophet˺ which of them can guarantee all that
t.ghali: Ask them, whichever of them will assertively guarantee that
t.irving: Ask them which of them will lay claim to that
t.iwq: Ask them which of them, for that claim, is responsible

t_id.kemenag: Tanyakanlah kepada mereka: "Siapakah di antara mereka yang bertanggung jawab terhadap keputusan yang diambil itu?"
t_id.sabiq: Tanyakan orang-orang musyrik, hai Muhammad, "Siapakah di antara mereka yang bertanggung jawab atas keputusan itu?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tanyakanlah kepada mereka, "Siapakah di antara mereka terhadap hal tersebut) hukum atau keputusan yang mereka ambil buat diri mereka sendiri, yaitu bahwasanya mereka kelak di akhirat akan diberi pahala yang lebih utama dari orang-orang mukmin (yang bertanggung jawab?") yang menanggungnya bagi mereka.
t_id.iwq: Tanyakanlah kepada mereka: "Siapakah di antara mereka yang bertanggung jawab terhadap keputusan yang diambil itu?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «سلهم أيهم بذلك» الحكم الذي يحكمون به لأنفسهم من أنهم يعطون في الآخرة أفضل من المؤمنين «زعيم» كفيل لهم
tafsir.siraj-ar: سَلۡهُمۡ أَيُّهُم بِذَٰلِكَ زَعِيمٌ (زَعِيمٌ: كَفِيلٌ وَضَامِنٌ بِأَنْ يَكُونَ لَهُمْ ذَلِكَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tanyakanlah kepada mereka, "Siapakah di antara mereka terhadap hal tersebut) hukum atau keputusan yang mereka ambil buat diri mereka sendiri, yaitu bahwasanya mereka kelak di akhirat akan diberi pahala yang lebih utama dari orang-orang mukmin (yang bertanggung jawab?") yang menanggungnya bagi mereka

ar: أَمۡ لَهُمۡ شُرَكَاۤءُ فَلۡیَأۡتُوا۟ بِشُرَكَاۤئِهِمۡ إِن كَانُوا۟ صَـٰدِقِینَ
ar_simple: أم لهم شركاء فلیأتوا بشركائهم إن كانوا صدقین

t.sahih-int: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful
t.asad: Or have they, perchance, anys ages to support their views? Well, then, if they are sincere in this their claim, let them produce those supporters of theirs
t.yusuf-ali: Or have they some "Partners" (in Allahhead)? Then let them produce their "partners", if they are truthful
t.pickthall: Or have they other gods? Then let them bring their other gods if they are truthful
t.arberry: Or do they have associates? Then let them bring their associates, if they speak truly
t.hilali-khan: Or have they "partners"? Then let them bring their "partners" if they are truthful
t.itani: Or do they have partners? Then let them produce their partners, if they are truthful.
t.mubarakpuri: Or have they "partners" Then let them bring their "partners" if they are truthful!
t.qarib: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they are telling the truth
t.wahiduddin: Or have they other partners [besides God]? Let them bring forth their other partners, if what they say be true
t.shakir: Or have they associates if they are truthful
t.haleem: Do they have ‘partners’ [besides God]? Let them produce their ‘partners’, if what they say is true
t.maariful: Or do they have associate-gods (who have guaranteed safety for them)? Then, let them bring their associate-gods, if they are true
t.khattab: Or do they have associate-gods ˹supporting this claim˺? Then let them bring forth their associate-gods, if what they say is true
t.ghali: Or even do they have associates (with Allah)? Then let them come up with their associates, in case they are sincere
t.irving: Or do they have associates [along with God]? Well, let them bring on their associates if they are so truthful
t.iwq: Or do they have partners? Then let them bring their partners, if they should be truthful

t_id.kemenag: Atau apakah mereka mempunyai sekutu-sekutu? Maka hendaklah mereka mendatangkan sekutu-sekutunya jika mereka adalah orang-orang yang benar.
t_id.sabiq: Atau apakah ada orang yang menyertai mereka dan mengikuti jalan mereka dalam ucapan tersebut? Datangkanlah sekutu-sekutu mereka jika mereka benar dalam tuduhan tersebut."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Atau apakah mereka mempunyai) di sisi mereka (sekutu-sekutu?) yang sepakat dengan mereka tentang ucapan itu, yaitu mereka akan menjamin bagi mereka dalam hal ini. Maka apabila memang demikian keadaannya (maka hendaklah mereka mendatangkan sekutu-sekutu mereka) yang akan memberikan jaminan tentang hal itu bagi mereka (jika mereka adalah orang-orang yang benar.)
t_id.iwq: Atau apakah mereka mempunyai sekutu-sekutu? Maka hendaklah mereka mendatangkan sekutu-sekutunya jika mereka adalah orang-orang yang benar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم لهم» أي عندهم «شركاء» موافقون لهم في هذا القول يكفلون به لهم فإن كان كذلك «فليأتوا بشركائهم» الكافلين لهم به «إن كانوا صادقين»
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ لَهُمۡ شُرَكَآءُ فَلۡيَأۡتُواْ بِشُرَكَآئِهِمۡ إِن كَانُواْ صَٰدِقِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Atau apakah mereka mempunyai) di sisi mereka (sekutu-sekutu?) yang sepakat dengan mereka tentang ucapan itu, yaitu mereka akan menjamin bagi mereka dalam hal ini. Maka apabila memang demikian keadaannya (maka hendaklah mereka mendatangkan sekutu-sekutu mereka) yang akan memberikan jaminan tentang hal itu bagi mereka (jika mereka adalah orang-orang yang benar)

ar: یَوۡمَ یُكۡشَفُ عَن سَاقࣲ وَیُدۡعَوۡنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا یَسۡتَطِیعُونَ
ar_simple: یوم یكشف عن ساق ویدعون إلى ٱلسجود فلا یستطیعون

t.sahih-int: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but the disbelievers will not be able
t.asad: on the Day when man's very being shall be bared to the bone, and when they [who now deny the truth] shall be called upon to prostrate themselves [before God], and shall be unable to do so
t.yusuf-ali: The Day that the shin shall be laid bare, and they shall be summoned to bow in adoration, but they shall not be able
t.pickthall: On the day when it befalleth in earnest, and they are ordered to prostrate themselves but are not able
t.arberry: Upon the day when the leg shall be bared, and they shall be summoned to bow themselves, but they cannot
t.hilali-khan: (Remember) the Day when the Shin shall be laid bare (i.e. the Day of Resurrection) and they shall be called to prostrate (to Allah), but they (hypocrites) shall not be able to do so
t.itani: On the Day when the Shin will be exposed, and they will be called to bow down, but they will be unable.
t.mubarakpuri: The Day when the Shin shall be laid bare and they shall be called to prostrate themselves, but they shall not be able to do so.
t.qarib: On the Day when legs are bared, they will be called to bow down, but will not be able
t.wahiduddin: On the Day when the truth shall be laid bare, they will be called upon to prostrate themselves, but they will not be able to do so
t.shakir: On the day when there shall be a severe affliction, and they shall be called upon to make obeisance, but they shall not be able
t.haleem: On the Day when matters become dire, they will be invited to prostrate themselves but will be prevented from doing so
t.maariful: On the Day when the Shin will be exposed, and they will be called upon to prostrate themselves, they will not be able to
t.khattab: ˹Beware of˺ the Day the Shin ˹of Allah˺ will be bared, and the wicked will be asked to prostrate, but they will not be able to do so
t.ghali: On the Day when the shank will be bared and they will be called to prostrate themselves, yet they are unable to
t.irving: Some day when their shinbone will be laid bare and they will be called upon to bow down on their knees, and they will not manage to
t.iwq: The Day the shin will be uncovered and they are invited to prostration but those who disbelieve will not be able

t_id.kemenag: Pada hari betis disingkapkan dan mereka dipanggil untuk bersujud; maka mereka tidak kuasa,
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari yang teramat sulit dan orang-orang kafir diseru untuk sujud--sebagai bentuk ejekan dan pelemahan--lalu mereka tidak mampu, pandangan mereka kabur dipenuhi oleh kehinaan yang sangat. Sesungguhnya dulu, ketika di dunia, mereka pernah diseru untuk bersujud. Tetapi mereka menolak untuk sujud, padahal mereka mampu melakukannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: Ingatlah (pada hari betis disingkapkan) ungkapan ini menggambarkan tentang dahsyatnya keadaan pada hari kiamat, yaitu sewaktu hisab dan pembalasan dilaksanakan. Dikatakan "kasyafatil harbu 'an saaqin", artinya perang itu berkobar dengan sengitnya (dan mereka dipanggil untuk bersujud) sebagai ujian buat keimanan mereka (maka mereka tidak kuasa) karena punggung mereka lekat bertumpuk menjadi satu, sehingga mereka tidak dapat bersujud.
t_id.iwq: Pada hari betis disingkapkan dan mereka dipanggil untuk bersujud; maka mereka tidak kuasa,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: اذكر «يوم يكشف عن ساق» هو عبارة عن شدة الأمر يوم القيامة للحساب والجزاء، يقال: كشفت الحرب عن ساق: إذا اشتد الأمر فيها «ويدعوْن إلى السجود» امتحاناً لإيمانهم «فلا يستطيعون» تصير ظهورهم طبقاً واحداً
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَوۡمَ يُكۡشَفُ عَن سَاقٖ وَيُدۡعَوۡنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ فَلَا يَسۡتَطِيعُونَ (يُكْشَفُ عَن سَاقٍ: يَكْشِفُ رَبُّنَا عَنْ سَاقِهِ؛ فَيَسْجُدُ المُؤْمِنُونَ، وَيَعْجِزُ المُنَافِقُونَ؛ كَمَا ثَبَتَ فِي الحَدِيثِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: Ingatlah (pada hari betis disingkapkan) ungkapan ini menggambarkan tentang dahsyatnya keadaan pada hari kiamat, yaitu sewaktu hisab dan pembalasan dilaksanakan. Dikatakan "kasyafatil harbu 'an saaqin", artinya perang itu berkobar dengan sengitnya (dan mereka dipanggil untuk bersujud) sebagai ujian buat keimanan mereka (maka mereka tidak kuasa) karena punggung mereka lekat bertumpuk menjadi satu, sehingga mereka tidak dapat bersujud

ar: خَـٰشِعَةً أَبۡصَـٰرُهُمۡ تَرۡهَقُهُمۡ ذِلَّةࣱۖ وَقَدۡ كَانُوا۟ یُدۡعَوۡنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمۡ سَـٰلِمُونَ
ar_simple: خشعة أبصرهم ترهقهم ذلة وقد كانوا یدعون إلى ٱلسجود وهم سلمون

t.sahih-int: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound
t.asad: downcast will be their eyes, with ignominy overwhelming them - seeing that they had been called upon [in vain] to prostrate themselves [before Him] while they were yet sound [and alive]
t.yusuf-ali: Their eyes will be cast down,- ignominy will cover them; seeing that they had been summoned aforetime to bow in adoration, while they were whole, (and had refused)
t.pickthall: With eyes downcast, abasement stupefying them. And they had been summoned to prostrate themselves while they were yet unhurt
t.arberry: humbled shall be their eyes, and abasement shall overspread them, for they had been summoned to bow themselves while they were whole
t.hilali-khan: Their eyes will be cast down, ignominy will cover them; they used to be called to prostrate (offer prayers), while they were healthy and good (in the life of the world, but they did not)
t.itani: Their eyes subdued, shame will cover them. They were invited to bow down when they were sound.
t.mubarakpuri: Their eyes will be cast down and ignominy will cover them; they used to be called to prostrate themselves, while they were.
t.qarib: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. They were invited to bow down while they were healthy
t.wahiduddin: Their eyes will be cast down and they will be covered in shame; they were bidden to prostrate themselves, when they were safe and sound [but they did not obey]
t.shakir: Their looks cast down, abasement shall overtake them; and they were called upon to make obeisance indeed while yet they were safe
t.haleem: and their eyes will be downcast and they will be overwhelmed with shame: they were invited to prostrate themselves when they were safe [but refused]
t.maariful: With their eyes downcast, they will be enveloped by ignominy. They used to be called upon to prostrate when they were healthy (but they did not)
t.khattab: with eyes downcast, totally covered with disgrace. For they were ˹always˺ called to prostrate ˹in the world˺ when they were fully capable ˹but they chose not to˺
t.ghali: Submissive will be their be holdings, (i.e., gazes) humbleness oppressing them. And they had been already called to prostrate themselves while (Literally: and) they were safe
t.irving: with their eyes cast down, disgrace will overwhelm them. They had been called upon to bow down on their knees while they still felt safe
t.iwq: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. And they used to be invited to prostration while they were sound

t_id.kemenag: (dalam keadaan) pandangan mereka tunduk ke bawah, lagi mereka diliputi kehinaan. Dan sesungguhnya mereka dahulu (di dunia) diseru untuk bersujud, dan mereka dalam keadaan sejahtera.
t_id.sabiq: Pada hari yang teramat sulit dan orang-orang kafir diseru untuk sujud--sebagai bentuk ejekan dan pelemahan--lalu mereka tidak mampu, pandangan mereka kabur dipenuhi oleh kehinaan yang sangat. Sesungguhnya dulu, ketika di dunia, mereka pernah diseru untuk bersujud. Tetapi mereka menolak untuk sujud, padahal mereka mampu melakukannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Seraya tunduk ke bawah) lafal khaasyi`atan ini merupakan hal dari dhamir yang terkandung pada lafal yad`uuna, artinya, dalam keadaan terhina (pandangan mereka) maksudnya, mereka tidak dapat mengangkat pandangan matanya (lagi mereka diliputi) mereka diselimuti (kehinaan. Dan sesungguhnya mereka dahulu diseru) sewaktu di dunia (untuk bersujud sedang mereka dalam keadaan sejahtera) akan tetapi mereka tidak mau melakukannya, yakni tidak mau salat.
t_id.iwq: (dalam keadaan) pandangan mereka tunduk ke bawah, lagi mereka diliputi kehinaan. Dan sesungguhnya mereka dahulu (di dunia) diseru untuk bersujud, dan mereka dalam keadaan sejahtera.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «خاشعة» حال من ضمير يدعون، أي ذليلة «أبصارهم» لا يرفعونها «ترهقهم» تغشاهم «ذلة وقد كانوا يدعوْن» في الدنيا «إلى السجود وهم سالمون» فلا يأتون به بأن لا يصلوا
tafsir.siraj-ar: خَٰشِعَةً أَبۡصَٰرُهُمۡ تَرۡهَقُهُمۡ ذِلَّةٞۖ وَقَدۡ كَانُواْ يُدۡعَوۡنَ إِلَى ٱلسُّجُودِ وَهُمۡ سَٰلِمُونَ (خَاشِعَةً أَبْصَارُهُمْ: مُنْكَسِرَةً ذَلِيلَةً؛ لَا يَرْفَعُونَهَا, تَرْهَقُهُمْ: تَغْشَاهُمْ, سَالِمُونَ: أَصِحَّاءُ، قَادِرُونَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Seraya tunduk ke bawah) lafal khaasyi`atan ini merupakan hal dari dhamir yang terkandung pada lafal yad`uuna, artinya, dalam keadaan terhina (pandangan mereka) maksudnya, mereka tidak dapat mengangkat pandangan matanya (lagi mereka diliputi) mereka diselimuti (kehinaan. Dan sesungguhnya mereka dahulu diseru) sewaktu di dunia (untuk bersujud sedang mereka dalam keadaan sejahtera) akan tetapi mereka tidak mau melakukannya, yakni tidak mau salat

ar: فَذَرۡنِی وَمَن یُكَذِّبُ بِهَـٰذَا ٱلۡحَدِیثِۖ سَنَسۡتَدۡرِجُهُم مِّنۡ حَیۡثُ لَا یَعۡلَمُونَ
ar_simple: فذرنی ومن یكذب بهذا ٱلحدیث سنستدرجهم من حیث لا یعلمون

t.sahih-int: So leave Me, [O Muhammad], with [the matter of] whoever denies the Qur'an. We will progressively lead them [to punishment] from where they do not know
t.asad: Hence, leave Me alone with such as give the lie to this tiding. We shall bring them low, step by step, without their perceiving how it has come about
t.yusuf-ali: Then leave Me alone with such as reject this Message: by degrees shall We punish them from directions they perceive not
t.pickthall: Leave Me (to deal) with those who give the lie to this pronouncement. We shall lead them on by steps from whence they know not
t.arberry: So leave Me with him who cries lies to this discourse! We will draw them on little by little whence they know not
t.hilali-khan: Then leave Me Alone with such as belie this Quran. We shall punish them gradually from directions they perceive not
t.itani: So leave Me to those who reject this discourse; We will proceed against them gradually, from where they do not know.
t.mubarakpuri: Then leave Me alone with whoever denies this narration. We shall punish them gradually from directions they perceive not.
t.qarib: So leave those who deny this revelation to Me. We will proceed against them gradually, from whence they do not know
t.wahiduddin: So leave to Me those who reject this message. We shall lead them step by step to their ruin, in ways beyond their ken
t.shakir: So leave Me and him who rejects this announcement; We will overtake them by degrees, from whence they perceive not
t.haleem: So [Prophet] leave those who reject this revelation to Me: We shall lead them on, step by step, in ways beyond their knowledge
t.maariful: So, leave Me alone with those who reject this discourse. We will draw them on little by little (towards Hell) from a way they do not know
t.khattab: So leave to Me ˹O Prophet˺ those who reject this message. We will gradually draw them to destruction in ways they cannot comprehend
t.ghali: So leave Me (to deal) with him who cries lies to this discourse! We will soon draw them gradually (to punishment) from where they do not know
t.irving: Leave Me Alone as well as anyone who rejects this account: We will lead them on gradually by means they do not recognize
t.iwq: So leave Me, O Muhammad, with the matter of whoever denies the Qur'an. We will progressively lead them to punishment from where they do not know

t_id.kemenag: Maka serahkanlah (ya Muhammad) kepada-Ku (urusan) orang-orang yang mendustakan perkataan ini (Al Quran). Nanti Kami akan menarik mereka dengan berangsur-angsur (ke arah kebinasaan) dari arah yang tidak mereka ketahui,
t_id.sabiq: Serahkan kepada-Ku, hai Muhammad, perkara orang yang mendustakan al-Qur'ân. Mereka akan Kami dekatkan ke siksa setahap demi setahap dari arah yang mereka tidak ketahui dari mana siksa itu datang.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka serahkanlah kepada-Ku) berikanlah kepada Aku (orang-orang yang mendustakan perkataan ini) yang mendustakan Alquran. (Nanti Kami akan menarik mereka dengan berangsur-angsur) Kami akan mengambil mereka secara berangsur-angsur (dari arah yang tidak mereka ketahui.)
t_id.iwq: Maka serahkanlah (ya Muhammad) kepada-Ku (urusan) orang-orang yang mendustakan perkataan ini (Al Al-Quran). Nanti Kami akan menarik mereka dengan berangsur-angsur (ke arah kebinasaan) dari arah yang tidak mereka ketahui,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فذرني» دعني «ومن يكذب بهذا الحديث» القرآن «سنستدرجهم» نأخذهم قليلا قليلا «من حيث لا يعلمون»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَذَرۡنِي وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهَٰذَا ٱلۡحَدِيثِۖ سَنَسۡتَدۡرِجُهُم مِّنۡ حَيۡثُ لَا يَعۡلَمُونَ (الْحَدِيثِ: القُرْآنِ, سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم: سَنَمُدُّهُمْ بِالأَمْوَالِ وَالنِّعَمِ؛ اسْتِدْرَاجًا لَهُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka serahkanlah kepada-Ku) berikanlah kepada Aku (orang-orang yang mendustakan perkataan ini) yang mendustakan Alquran. (Nanti Kami akan menarik mereka dengan berangsur-angsur) Kami akan mengambil mereka secara berangsur-angsur (dari arah yang tidak mereka ketahui)

t.sahih-int: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm
t.asad: for, behold, though I may give them rein for a while, My subtle scheme is exceedingly firm
t.yusuf-ali: A (long) respite will I grant them: truly powerful is My Plan
t.pickthall: Yet I bear with them, for lo! My scheme is firm
t.arberry: and I shall respite them -- assuredly My guile is sure
t.hilali-khan: And I will grant them a respite. Verily, My Plan is strong
t.itani: And I will give them respite. My plan is firm.
t.mubarakpuri: And I will grant them a respite. Verily, My plan is strong.
t.qarib: And I will give them time. My plan is firm
t.wahiduddin: I shall grant them some respite, for My plan is powerful
t.shakir: And I do bear with them, surely My plan is firm
t.haleem: I will allow them more time, for My plan is powerful
t.maariful: And I give them respite. Indeed, My plan is firm
t.khattab: I ˹only˺ delay their end for a while, but My planning is flawless
t.ghali: And I will reprieve them. Surely My plotting is ever-pervading
t.irving: I shall even put up with them for a while; for My plan is certain
t.iwq: And I will give them time. Indeed, My plan is firm

t_id.kemenag: dan Aku memberi tangguh kepada mereka. Sesungguhnya rencana-Ku amat tangguh.
t_id.sabiq: Aku memberi mereka tangguh dengan menunda siksa. Sesungguhnya rencana-Ku amat teguh dan tak seorang pun akan luput darinya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Aku memberi tangguh kepada mereka) Aku menangguhkan mereka. (Sesungguhnya rencana-Ku amat teguh) amat kuat dan tak dapat ditinggalkan.
t_id.iwq: dan Aku memberi tangguh kepada mereka. Sesungguhnya rencana-Ku amat tangguh.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأملي لهم» أمهلهم «إن كيدي متين» شديد لا يطاق
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأُمۡلِي لَهُمۡۚ إِنَّ كَيۡدِي مَتِينٌ (وَأُمْلِي لَهُمْ: أُمْهِلُهُمْ، وَأُطِيلُ أَعْمَارَهُمْ, مَتِينٌ: قَوِيٌّ، شَدِيدٌ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Aku memberi tangguh kepada mereka) Aku menangguhkan mereka. (Sesungguhnya rencana-Ku amat teguh) amat kuat dan tak dapat ditinggalkan

ar: أَمۡ تَسۡءَلُهُمۡ أَجۡرࣰا فَهُم مِّن مَّغۡرَمࣲ مُّثۡقَلُونَ
ar_simple: أم تسءلهم أجرا فهم من مغرم مثقلون

t.sahih-int: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down
t.asad: Or is it that [they fear lest] thou ask them for a reward, [O Prophet,] so that they would be burdened with debt [if they listened to thee]
t.yusuf-ali: Or is it that thou dost ask them for a reward, so that they are burdened with a load of debt
t.pickthall: Or dost thou (Muhammad) ask a fee from them so that they are heavily taxed
t.arberry: Or askest thou them for a wage, and so they are weighed down with debt
t.hilali-khan: Or is it that you (O Muhammad SAW) ask them a wage, so that they are heavily burdened with debt
t.itani: Or do you ask them for a fee, so they are burdened with debt?
t.mubarakpuri: Or is it that you ask them a wage, so that they are heavily burdened with debt
t.qarib: Or do you ask them for a wage, so they are burdened with debt
t.wahiduddin: Do you demand some recompense from them that would weigh them down with debt
t.shakir: Or do you ask from them a reward, so that they are burdened with debt
t.haleem: Do you demand some reward from them that would burden them with debt
t.maariful: Is it that you ask them for a fee, due to which they are burdened with debt
t.khattab: Or are you asking them for a reward ˹for the message˺ so that they are overburdened by debt
t.ghali: Or even do you ask them for a reward so they are weighed down with heavy fines
t.irving: Or are you asking them for some fee while they are weighted down with debt
t.iwq: Or do you ask of them a payment, so they are by debt burdened down

t_id.kemenag: Apakah kamu meminta upah kepada mereka, lalu mereka diberati dengan hutang?
t_id.sabiq: Ataukah kamu meminta upah dari mereka sebagai imbalan menyampaikan risalah sehingga mereka merasa terbebani untuk membayar upah tersebut?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ataukah) yakni apakah (kamu meminta kepada mereka) atas penyampaian risalahmu (upah dengan utang, lalu mereka karena utang itu) karena apa yang harus mereka bayarkan kepadamu (merasa keberatan) karena itu lalu mereka tidak mau beriman kepada Alquran.
t_id.iwq: Apakah kamu meminta upah kepada mereka, lalu mereka diberati dengan hutang?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم» بل أ «تسألهم» على تبليغ الرسالة «أجرا فهم من مغرم» مما يعطونكه «مثقلون» فلا يؤمنون لذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ تَسۡـَٔلُهُمۡ أَجۡرٗا فَهُم مِّن مَّغۡرَمٖ مُّثۡقَلُونَ (مَّغْرَمٍ: غَرَامَةِ تِلْكَ الأُجْرَةِ, مُّثْقَلُونَ: مُكَلَّفُونَ حِمْلًا ثَقِيلًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ataukah) yakni apakah (kamu meminta kepada mereka) atas penyampaian risalahmu (upah dengan utang, lalu mereka karena utang itu) karena apa yang harus mereka bayarkan kepadamu (merasa keberatan) karena itu lalu mereka tidak mau beriman kepada Alquran

t.sahih-int: Or have they [knowledge of] the unseen, so they write [it] down
t.asad: Or [do they think] that the hidden reality [of all that exists] is within their grasp, so that [in time] they can write it down
t.yusuf-ali: Or that the Unseen is in their hands, so that they can write it down
t.pickthall: Or is the Unseen theirs that they can write (thereof)
t.arberry: Or is the Unseen in their keeping, and so they are writing it down
t.hilali-khan: Or that the Ghaib (unseen here in this Verse it means Al-Lauh Al-Mahfuz) is in their hands, so that they can write it down
t.itani: Or do they know the future, and so they write it down?
t.mubarakpuri: Or that the Unseen is in their hands, so that they can write it down
t.qarib: Or do they know the future, and they write what is going to happen
t.wahiduddin: Is the unseen within their grasp so that they write it down
t.shakir: Or have they (the knowledge of) the unseen, so that they write (it) down
t.haleem: Do they have knowledge of the unseen that enables them to write it down
t.maariful: Or do they have the (knowledge of the) Unseen, and they write it down
t.khattab: Or do they have access to ˹the Record in˺ the unseen, so they copy it ˹for all to see˺
t.ghali: Or even is the Unseen in their presence so they are writing (it down)
t.irving: Or do they hold the Unseen which they are writing down
t.iwq: Or have they knowledge of the unseen, so they write it down

t_id.kemenag: Ataukah ada pada mereka ilmu tentang yang ghaib lalu mereka menulis (padanya apa yang mereka tetapkan)?
t_id.sabiq: Ataukah mereka memiliki pengetahuan tentang yang gaib sehingga mereka mampu menulis apa yang mereka putuskan?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ataukah ada pada mereka ilmu tentang yang gaib) yaitu mengenai apa yang tertulis di Lohmahfuz (lalu mereka menulis) daripadanya apa yang mereka katakan itu.
t_id.iwq: Ataukah ada pada mereka ilmu tentang yang ghaib lalu mereka menulis (padanya apa yang mereka tetapkan)?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أم عندهم الغيب» أي اللوح المحفوظ الذي فيه الغيب «فهم يكتبون» منه ما يقولون
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَمۡ عِندَهُمُ ٱلۡغَيۡبُ فَهُمۡ يَكۡتُبُونَ (أَمْ عِندَهُمُ: بَلْ أَعِنْدَهُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ataukah ada pada mereka ilmu tentang yang gaib) yaitu mengenai apa yang tertulis di Lohmahfuz (lalu mereka menulis) daripadanya apa yang mereka katakan itu
