ar: فَٱصۡبِرۡ لِحُكۡمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلۡحُوتِ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكۡظُومࣱ
ar_simple: فٱصبر لحكم ربك ولا تكن كصاحب ٱلحوت إذ نادى وهو مكظوم

t.sahih-int: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, [O Muhammad], and be not like the companion of the fish when he called out while he was distressed
t.asad: BEAR THEN with patience thy Sustainer's will and be not like him of the great fish, who cried out [in distress] after having given in to anger
t.yusuf-ali: So wait with patience for the Command of thy Lord, and be not like the Companion of the Fish,- when he cried out in agony
t.pickthall: But wait thou for thy Lord's decree, and be not like him of the fish, who cried out in despair
t.arberry: So be thou patient under the judgment of thy Lord, and be not as the Man of the Fish, when he called, choking inwardly
t.hilali-khan: So wait with patience for the Decision of your Lord, and be not like the Companion of the Fish, when he cried out (to Us) while he was in deep sorrow. (See the Quran, Verse)
t.itani: So wait patiently for the Decision of your Lord, and do not be like the Fellow of the Fish who cried out in despair.
t.mubarakpuri: So, wait with patience for the decision of your Lord, and be not like the Companion of the Fish when he cried out (to Us) while he was Makzum.
t.qarib: So be patient for your Lord's judgment, and do not be like the fellow of the whale who called out in despair
t.wahiduddin: Wait patiently for your Lord's judgement; do not be like the man who, having been swallowed by a whale, called out in distress
t.shakir: So wait patiently for the judgment of your Lord, and be not like the companion of the fish, when he cried while he was in distress
t.haleem: Wait patiently [Prophet] for your Lord’s judgement: do not be like the man in the whale who called out in distress
t.maariful: So, remain patient with your Lord’s judgment, and be not like the Man of the Fish, he cried out while he was in anguish
t.khattab: So be patient with your Lord’s decree, and do not be like ˹Jonah,˺ the Man of the Whale, who cried out ˹to Allah˺, in total distress
t.ghali: So (endure) patiently under the Judgment of your Lord, and do not be as the Companion of the Whale (Prophet Yunus "Jonah") as he called out, as he (was) made to choke (inwardly)
t.irving: Be patient with your Lord´s decision and not like the Whale´s Companion when he called out as he felt stifled
t.iwq: Then be patient for the decision of your Lord, O Muhammad, and be not like the companion of the fish when he called out while he was distressed

t_id.kemenag: Maka bersabarlah kamu (hai Muhammad) terhadap ketetapan Tuhanmu, dan janganlah kamu seperti orang yang berada dalam (perut) ikan ketika ia berdoa sedang ia dalam keadaan marah (kepada kaumnya).
t_id.sabiq: Bersabarlah atas penangguhan yang diberikan kepada mereka dan penundaan bantuan kemenangan kepadamu. Jangan seperti Yûnus--yang dimakan ikan hiu--yang tidak sabar dan marah kepada kaumnya ketika ia menyeru kepada Tuhan-Nya dalam keadaan marah seraya meminta agar azab mereka disegerakan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka bersabarlah kamu terhadap ketetapan Rabbmu) terhadap mereka, sesuai dengan apa yang dikehendaki-Nya (dan janganlah kamu seperti orang yang berada dalam perut ikan paus) dalam hal ketergesa-gesaannya dan ketidaksabarannya, yaitu sebagaimana Nabi Yunus a.s. (ketika ia berdoa) kepada Rabbnya (sedangkan ia dalam keadaan marah) terhadap kaumnya, hatinya penuh dengan kemarahan sewaktu ia berada di dalam perut ikan besar itu.
t_id.iwq: Maka bersabarlah kamu (hai Muhammad) terhadap ketetapan Tuhanmu, dan janganlah kamu seperti orang yang berada dalam (perut) ikan ketika ia berdoa sedang ia dalam keadaan marah (kepada kaumnya).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فاصبر لحكم ربك» فيهم بما يشاء «ولا تكن كصاحب الحوت» في الضجر والعجلة وهو يونس عليه السلام «إذ نادى» دعا ربه «وهو مكظوم» مملوء غما في بطن الحوت
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَٱصۡبِرۡ لِحُكۡمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ ٱلۡحُوتِ إِذۡ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكۡظُومٞ (وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ الْحُوتِ: لَا تَكُنْ مِثْلَ يُونُسَ حِينَ اسْتَعْجَلَ العَذَابَ، وَغَضِبَ, مَكْظُومٌ: مَمْلُوءٌ غَمًّا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka bersabarlah kamu terhadap ketetapan Rabbmu) terhadap mereka, sesuai dengan apa yang dikehendaki-Nya (dan janganlah kamu seperti orang yang berada dalam perut ikan paus) dalam hal ketergesa-gesaannya dan ketidaksabarannya, yaitu sebagaimana Nabi Yunus a.s. (ketika ia berdoa) kepada Rabbnya (sedangkan ia dalam keadaan marah) terhadap kaumnya, hatinya penuh dengan kemarahan sewaktu ia berada di dalam perut ikan besar itu

ar: لَّوۡلَاۤ أَن تَدَ ٰرَكَهُۥ نِعۡمَةࣱ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلۡعَرَاۤءِ وَهُوَ مَذۡمُومࣱ
ar_simple: لولا أن تد ركه نعمة من ربه لنبذ بٱلعراء وهو مذموم

t.sahih-int: If not that a favor from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured
t.asad: [And remember:] had not grace from his Sustainer reached him, he would indeed have been cast forth upon that barren shore in a state of disgrace
t.yusuf-ali: Had not Grace from his Lord reached him, he would indeed have been cast off on the naked shore, in disgrace
t.pickthall: Had it not been that favour from his Lord had reached him he surely had been cast into the wilderness while he was reprobate
t.arberry: Had there not overtaken him a blessing from his Lord he would have been cast upon the wilderness, being condemned
t.hilali-khan: Had not a Grace from his Lord reached him, he would indeed have been (left in the stomach of the fish, but We forgave him), so he was cast off on the naked shore, while he was to be blamed
t.itani: Were it not for his Lord’s favor that reached him, he would have been thrown into the wilderness, fully despised.
t.mubarakpuri: Had not a grace from his Lord reached him, he would indeed have been (left in the stomach of the fish, but We forgave him), so he was cast off on the naked shore, while he was to be blamed.
t.qarib: Had a blessing from his Lord not reached him, he would have been abandoned and disgraced on a naked shore
t.wahiduddin: Had his Lord's grace not been bestowed upon him, he would have been cast away in disgrace upon that desolate shore
t.shakir: Were it not that favor from his Lord had overtaken him, he would certainly have been cast down upon the naked Found while he was blamed
t.haleem: if his Lord’s grace had not reached him, he would have been left, abandoned and blameworthy, on the barren shore
t.maariful: Had not a favour from His Lord come to his help, he would have been cast in the wilderness in a reproachable state
t.khattab: Had he not been shown grace by his Lord, he would have certainly been cast onto the open ˹shore˺, still blameworthy
t.ghali: Had there not overtaken him a favor from his Lord, indeed he would have been flung off upon the naked shore (i.e., the wilderness) (while) he was a reprobate
t.irving: If favor from his Lord had not reached him, he would have been flung upon the empty beach while he was still to blame
t.iwq: If not that a favor from his Lord overtook him, he would have been thrown onto the naked shore while he was censured

t_id.kemenag: Kalau sekiranya ia tidak segera mendapat nikmat dari Tuhannya, benar-benar ia dicampakkan ke tanah tandus dalam keadaan tercela.
t_id.sabiq: Kalau tidak segera mendapat nikmat Tuhan berupa penerimaan tobatnya, niscaya ia sudah dilemparkan dari perut hiu ke suatu padang luas dalam keadaan tersiksa karena kesalahannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Kalau sekiranya ia tidak segera mendapat) tidak segera disusul oleh (nikmat) yakni rahmat (dari Rabbnya, benar-benar ia dicampakkan) dari perut ikan besar itu (ke tanah yang tandus) tanah yang tidak ada tumbuh-tumbuhannya (dalam keadaan tercela) akan tetapi Allah mengasihaninya sehingga ia dicampakkan tidak dalam keadaan tercela.
t_id.iwq: Kalau sekiranya ia tidak segera mendapat nikmat dari Tuhannya, benar-benar ia dicampakkan ke tanah tandus dalam keadaan tercela.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «لولا أن تداركه» أدركه «نعمة» رحمة «من ربه لنبذ» من بطن الحوت «بالعراء» بالأرض الفضاء «وهو مذموم» لكنه رحم فنبذ غير مذموم
tafsir.siraj-ar: لَّوۡلَآ أَن تَدَٰرَكَهُۥ نِعۡمَةٞ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ لَنُبِذَ بِٱلۡعَرَآءِ وَهُوَ مَذۡمُومٞ (نِعْمَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِ: بِتَوْفِيقِهِ لِلتَّوْبَةِ، وَقَبُولِهَا, لَنُبِذَ بِالْعَرَاءِ: لَطُرِحَ مِنْ بَطْنِ الحُوتِ بِالأَرْضِ الفَضَاءِ المُهْلِكَةِ, وَهُوَ مَذْمُومٌ: آتٍ بِمَا يُلَامُ عَلَيْهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Kalau sekiranya ia tidak segera mendapat) tidak segera disusul oleh (nikmat) yakni rahmat (dari Rabbnya, benar-benar ia dicampakkan) dari perut ikan besar itu (ke tanah yang tandus) tanah yang tidak ada tumbuh-tumbuhannya (dalam keadaan tercela) akan tetapi Allah mengasihaninya sehingga ia dicampakkan tidak dalam keadaan tercela

t.sahih-int: And his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous
t.asad: but [as it was,] his Sustainer had elected him and placed him among the righteous
t.yusuf-ali: Thus did his Lord choose him and make him of the Company of the Righteous
t.pickthall: But his Lord chose him and placed him among the righteous
t.arberry: But his Lord had chosen him, and He placed him among the righteous
t.hilali-khan: But his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous
t.itani: But his Lord chose him, and made him one of the righteous.
t.mubarakpuri: Then his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous.
t.qarib: But his Lord chose him and made him among the righteous
t.wahiduddin: But his Lord chose him for His own and made him one of the righteous
t.shakir: Then his Lord chose him, and He made him of the good
t.haleem: but his Lord chose him and made him one of the Righteous
t.maariful: Then his Lord chose him, and made him one of the righteous
t.khattab: Then his Lord chose him, making him one of the righteous
t.ghali: Then his Lord selected him and so made him among the righteous
t.irving: His Lord picked him out and placed him with honorable people
t.iwq: And his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous

t_id.kemenag: Lalu Tuhannya memilihnya dan menjadikannya termasuk orang-orang yang saleh.
t_id.sabiq: Lalu Tuhan memilihnya dengan menerima tobatnya dan menjadikannya termasuk orang-orang yang saleh.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Lalu Rabbnya memilihnya) memberinya kenabian (dan menjadikannya termasuk orang-orang yang saleh) yakni sebagian dari para nabi.
t_id.iwq: Lalu Tuhannya memilihnya dan menjadikannya termasuk orang-orang yang saleh.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فاجتباه ربه» بالنبوة «فجعله من الصالحين» الأنبياء
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَٱجۡتَبَٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَجَعَلَهُۥ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (فَاجْتَبَاهُ: اصْطَفَاهُ رَبُّهُ لِرِسَالَتِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Lalu Rabbnya memilihnya) memberinya kenabian (dan menjadikannya termasuk orang-orang yang saleh) yakni sebagian dari para nabi

ar: وَإِن یَكَادُ ٱلَّذِینَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَیُزۡلِقُونَكَ بِأَبۡصَـٰرِهِمۡ لَمَّا سَمِعُوا۟ ٱلذِّكۡرَ وَیَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجۡنُونࣱ
ar_simple: وإن یكاد ٱلذین كفروا لیزلقونك بأبصرهم لما سمعوا ٱلذكر ویقولون إنه لمجنون

t.sahih-int: And indeed, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad
t.asad: Hence, [be patient,] even though they who are bent on denying the truth would all but kill thee with their eyes whenever they hear this reminder, and [though] they say, "[As for Mubammad,] behold, most surely he is a madman
t.yusuf-ali: And the Unbelievers would almost trip thee up with their eyes when they hear the Message; and they say: "Surely he is possessed
t.pickthall: And lo! those who disbelieve would fain disconcert thee with their eyes when they hear the Reminder, and they say: Lo! he is indeed mad
t.arberry: The unbelievers wellnigh strike thee down with their glances, when they hear the Reminder, and they say, 'Surely he is a man possessed
t.hilali-khan: And verily, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes through hatredness when they hear the Reminder (the Quran), and they say: "Verily, he (Muhammad SAW) is a madman
t.itani: Those who disbelieve almost stab you with their glances when they hear the message, and say, “He is crazy!”
t.mubarakpuri: And verily, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes (through hatred) when they hear the Reminder (the Qur'an), and they say: "Verily, he is a madman!"
t.qarib: Those unbelievers all but kill you with their evil looks when they hear the message, and they say, "He must be mad
t.wahiduddin: When those who deny the truth hear the admonition, they would almost cause you to stumble with their evil eyes; and they say, "He is certainly mad
t.shakir: And those who disbelieve would almost smite you with their eyes when they hear the reminder, and they say: Most surely he is mad
t.haleem: The disbelievers almost strike you down with their looks when they hear the Quran. They say, ‘He must be mad!’
t.maariful: And indeed the disbelievers seem to trip you up with their glances when they hear the Reminder, and say, “He is a madman indeed.”
t.khattab: The disbelievers would almost cut you down with their eyes when they hear ˹you recite˺ the Reminder, and say, “He is certainly a madman.”
t.ghali: And decidedly the ones who have disbelieved were indeed almost about to trip you with their be holdings as soon as they heard the Remembrance, and they say, "Surely he is indeed a madman
t.irving: The ones who disbelieve almost trip you up by glaring at you whenever they hear the Reminder, and they say: "He´s deranged
t.iwq: And verily, those who disbelieve would almost make you slip with their eyes when they hear the message, and they say, "Indeed, he is mad

t_id.kemenag: Dan sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir itu benar-benar hampir menggelincirkan kamu dengan pandangan mereka, tatkala mereka mendengar Al Quran dan mereka berkata: "Sesungguhnya ia (Muhammad) benar-benar orang yang gila".
t_id.sabiq: Orang-orang kafir itu benar-benar hampir mengenyahkanmu dari tempatmu dengan pandangan marah dan bermusuhan tatkala mereka mendengar al-Qur'ân seraya mengatakan, "Sesungguhnya kamu benar- benar gila."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir itu benar-benar hampir menggelincirkan kamu) dapat dibaca layuzliquunaka dan layazliquunaka (dengan pandangan mereka) mereka memandangmu dengan pandangan yang sangat tajam, sehingga pandangannya hampir-hampir membuatmu pingsan dan menjatuhkanmu dari tempat atau kedudukanmu (tatkala mereka mendengar peringatan) yakni Alquran (dan mereka berkata) karena dengki, ("Sesungguhnya ia benar-benar orang gila.") Mereka dengki karena Alquran yang diturunkan kepadanya itu.
t_id.iwq: Dan sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir itu benar-benar hampir menggelincirkan kamu dengan pandangan mereka, tatkala mereka mendengar Al Al-Quran dan mereka berkata: "Sesungguhnya ia (Muhammad) benar-benar orang yang gila".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وإن يكاد الذين كفروا ليزلقونك» بضم الياء وفتحها «بأبصارهم» ينظرون إليك نظرا شديدا يكاد أن يصرعك ويسقطك من مكانك «لما سمعوا الذكر» القرآن «ويقولون» حسدا «إنه لمجنون» بسبب القرآن الذي جاء به
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِن يَكَادُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَيُزۡلِقُونَكَ بِأَبۡصَٰرِهِمۡ لَمَّا سَمِعُواْ ٱلذِّكۡرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُۥ لَمَجۡنُونٞ (لَيُزْلِقُونَكَ: لَيُسْقِطُونَكَ عَنْ مَكَانِكَ؛ بِنَظَرِهِمْ إِلَيْكَ؛ عَدَاوَةً وَبُغْضًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan sesungguhnya orang-orang kafir itu benar-benar hampir menggelincirkan kamu) dapat dibaca layuzliquunaka dan layazliquunaka (dengan pandangan mereka) mereka memandangmu dengan pandangan yang sangat tajam, sehingga pandangannya hampir-hampir membuatmu pingsan dan menjatuhkanmu dari tempat atau kedudukanmu (tatkala mereka mendengar peringatan) yakni Alquran (dan mereka berkata) karena dengki, ("Sesungguhnya ia benar-benar orang gila.") Mereka dengki karena Alquran yang diturunkan kepadanya itu

t.sahih-int: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds
t.asad: [Be patient:] for this is nought else but a reminder [from God] to all mankind
t.yusuf-ali: But it is nothing less than a Message to all the worlds
t.pickthall: When it is naught else than a Reminder to creation
t.arberry: And it is nothing but a Reminder unto all beings
t.hilali-khan: But it is nothing else than a Reminder to all the 'Alamin (mankind, jinns and all that exists)
t.itani: But it is no less than a reminder to all the Worlds.
t.mubarakpuri: But it is nothing else than a Reminder to all the creatures (`Alamin).
t.qarib: But it is only a reminder to all people
t.wahiduddin: Yet it is purely an admonition to mankind
t.shakir: And it is naught but a reminder to the nations
t.haleem: but truly it is nothing other than a Reminder for all peoples
t.maariful: And it is nothing else but a Reminder for all the worlds
t.khattab: But it is simply a reminder to the whole world
t.ghali: And in no way is it anything except a Remembrance to the worlds
t.irving: Yet it is merely a Reminder to [everybody in] the Universe
t.iwq: But it is not except a reminder to the worlds

t_id.kemenag: Dan Al Quran itu tidak lain hanyalah peringatan bagi seluruh umat.
t_id.sabiq: Al-Qur'ân tidak lain hanyalah nasehat, pelajaran dan peringatan bagi seluruh alam.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidak lain dia) yakni Alquran itu (hanyalah peringatan) pelajaran (bagi seluruh umat) yaitu jin dan manusia, dan tiada menimbulkan kegilaan disebabkannya.
t_id.iwq: Dan Al Al-Quran itu tidak lain hanyalah peringatan bagi seluruh umat.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وما هو» أي القرآن «إلا ذكر» موعظة «للعالمين» الجن والإنس لا يحدث بسبب جنون
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَمَا هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكۡرٞ لِّلۡعَٰلَمِينَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan tidak lain dia) yakni Alquran itu (hanyalah peringatan) pelajaran (bagi seluruh umat) yaitu jin dan manusia, dan tiada menimbulkan kegilaan disebabkannya
